heightLength.es: Alrededor de 4–5 pies hasta el hombro

basic.en: Cows have four stomachs, so they can turn grass into energy like a super-powered salad factory!
basic.tl: May apat na tiyan ang baka, kaya ginagawang enerhiya ang damo parang super factory ng salad!
basic.es: ¡Las vacas tienen cuatro estómagos y convierten el pasto en energía como una fábrica de ensaladas!

edu.en: Cattle are ruminants that chew cud, breaking down tough plants with help from microbes in their gut.
edu.tl: Ang mga baka ay ruminante na ngumunguya ng cud; tinutulungan sila ng mikrobyo sa tiyan na sirain ang matitigas na halaman.
edu.es: El ganado rumia y recicla su comida; microbios en el estómago ayudan a descomponer plantas duras.

region.tl: Sa buong mundo sa mga bukid
region.es: En granjas de todo el mundo

heightLength.tl: Mga 2–3 ft ang taas sa balikat
heightLength.es: Alrededor de 2–3 pies de alto al hombro

basic.en: Pigs are super smart—some studies say they learn tricks as fast as dogs and even enjoy playtime!
basic.tl: Napakatalino ng baboy—natututo sila ng tricks kasing bilis ng aso at gustong maglaro!
basic.es: ¡Los cerdos son muy inteligentes; aprenden trucos casi tan rápido como los perros y les encanta jugar!

edu.en: Pigs use their sensitive snouts to root in soil and find food, acting like living plows and detectors.
edu.tl: Ginagamit ng baboy ang maliit na ilong para mag-ukit sa lupa at hanapin ang pagkain—parang plow at detector!
edu.es: Los cerdos usan el hocico para hurgar en la tierra y encontrar comida, como arados y detectores vivientes.

habitat.es: Granjas, establos y charcos de barro

region.tl: Sa buong mundo; may mga kamag-anak sa gubat

basic.en: Chickens can remember over 100 faces—including yours—and they talk to each other with more than 30 different clucks!
basic.tl: Natatandaan ng manok ang higit 100 mukha—kasama ka—at may 30+ uri ng tunog para mag-usap!
basic.es: ¡Las gallinas recuerdan más de 100 caras y se comunican con más de 30 tipos de cacareos!

edu.en: Hens lay eggs from one ovary; the hard shell forms in about 24 hours from calcium in their diet.
edu.tl: Nangingitlog ang inahing manok mula sa isang obaryo; nabubuo ang shell sa ~24 oras mula sa calcium.
edu.es: Las gallinas ponen huevos desde un ovario; la cáscara dura se forma en unas 24 horas con calcio de su dieta.

region.tl: Sa buong mundo sa mga bukid
region.es: En granjas de todo el mundo

basic.en: Horses can sleep standing up thanks to a special lock in their legs—like built-in kickstands!
basic.tl: Natutulog ang kabayo nakatayo dahil sa espesyal na lock sa binti—parang kickstand!
basic.es: ¡Los caballos pueden dormir de pie gracias a un cerrojo especial en las patas, como un caballete!

edu.en: Horses have the largest eyes of any land mammal, giving them almost 360° vision to spot predators.
edu.tl: May pinakamalaking mata ang kabayo sa lahat ng mammal sa lupa—halos 360° ang paningin!
edu.es: Los caballos tienen los ojos más grandes de los mamíferos terrestres y casi visión de 360°.

region.en: Worldwide; many breeds on every continent
region.tl: Sa buong mundo; maraming lahi sa bawat kontinente
region.es: Mundial; muchas razas en todos los continentes

basic.en: Goats are amazing climbers—they can scale steep cliffs and even balance on tiny ledges!
basic.tl: Mahusay umakyat ang kambing—kayang akyatin ang matarik na bangin at mag-balance sa maliit na ledge!
basic.es: ¡Las cabras trepan acantilados empinados y equilibran en pequeñas repisas!

edu.en: Goats have rectangular pupils that give them wide panoramic vision to watch for predators on mountains.
edu.tl: May parihabang pupil ang kambing para malawak ang paningin at makita ang mangangaso sa bundok.
edu.es: Las cabras tienen pupilas rectangulares que les dan visión panorámica en la montaña.

region.tl: Sa buong mundo; kilala sa bundok

basic.en: Sheep can remember faces for years, so a friendly “baa” today might still get recognized long after you’ve grown taller.
basic.tl: Kayang tandaan ng tupa ang mukha nang maraming taon, kaya ang friendly na “baa” ngayon ay puwedeng maalala kahit tumangkad ka na.
basic.es: Las ovejas recuerdan caras por años, así que un “bee” amistoso hoy puede ser reconocido mucho después, cuando ya hayas crecido.

edu.en: Wool grows from hair follicles in the skin; shearers clip it without hurting the sheep, like a haircut.
edu.tl: Lumalaki ang lana mula sa balahibo sa balat; ginupit ito ng shearer nang hindi nasasaktan ang tupa.
edu.es: La lana crece en folículos; los esquiladores la cortan sin dañar a la oveja, como un corte de pelo.

region.tl: Sa buong mundo sa mga bukid
region.es: En granjas de todo el mundo

basic.en: Ducks have waterproof feathers—special oil from a gland near the tail keeps them dry underwater!
basic.tl: May waterproof na balahibo ang pato—espesyal na langis mula sa glandula malapit sa buntot ang nagpapanatiling tuyo!
basic.es: ¡Las plumas de los patos son impermeables gracias al aceite de una glándula cerca de la cola!

edu.en: Ducks have a comb-like structure inside the bill that filters food from water and mud.
edu.tl: May parang suklay sa loob ng tuka ng pato na nagfi-filter ng pagkain mula sa tubig at putik.
edu.es: Los patos tienen lamelas en el pico que filtran comida del agua y el lodo.

region.tl: Sa buong mundo malapit sa tubig-tabang

diet.tl: Halaman, buto, at maliliit na hayop (omniboro)

heightLength.en: About 3–4 ft long with tail spread
heightLength.tl: Mga 3–4 ft haba kapag nakabuka ang buntot
heightLength.es: Alrededor de 3–4 pies con la cola extendida

basic.en: Male turkeys puff up and fan their colorful tail feathers to impress friends—and rivals!
basic.tl: Pinapalaki ng lalaking turkey ang katawan at ibinubuka ang makulay na buntot para mag-impress!
basic.es: ¡Los pavos machos inflan plumas y abren la cola colorida para impresionar!

edu.en: Turkeys can fly short bursts up to 55 mph and roost in trees at night to stay safe.
edu.tl: Kaya lumipad ng maikli ang turkey hanggang 55 mph at natutulog sa puno gabi-gabi para ligtas.
edu.es: Los pavos vuelan en ráfagas hasta 55 mph y duermen en árboles por la noche.

region.tl: Hilagang Amerika; inaalagaan sa buong mundo
region.es: Norteamérica; criados en todo el mundo

basic.en: A rooster’s sunrise crow can travel far, like a feathered alarm clock announcing morning to the whole neighborhood.
basic.tl: Umaabot nang malayo ang tilaok ng tandang sa umaga, parang feathered alarm clock na nagsasabing umaga na sa buong paligid.
basic.es: El canto del gallo al amanecer viaja lejos, como un despertador con plumas que anuncia la mañana a todo el vecindario.

edu.en: Roosters have sharp spurs on their legs used in dominance displays and to protect the flock.
edu.tl: May matutulis na spur sa binti ang tandang para sa dominansya at proteksyon ng kawan.
edu.es: Los gallos tienen espolones en las patas para dominancia y proteger al grupo.

region.tl: Sa buong mundo sa mga bukid
region.es: En granjas de todo el mundo

basic.en: Donkeys have huge ears that work like satellite dishes—they hear danger from far away!
basic.tl: Malalaki ang tainga ng asno—parang satellite dish para marinig ang panganib mula malayo!
basic.es: ¡Las orejas enormes del burro captan sonidos lejanos como antenas!

edu.en: Donkeys are sure-footed on rocky trails and have been trusted pack animals for thousands of years.
edu.tl: Matatag ang asno sa batong daan at pinagkakatiwalaang pack animal sa loob ng libo-libong taon.
edu.es: Los burros son seguros en senderos rocosos y llevan cargas desde hace miles de años.

region.tl: Aprika, Asya, at bukid sa buong mundo

basic.en: Llamas hum when they’re happy, a soft song that sounds like a fuzzy friend saying ‘life is good today.’
basic.tl: Umuungal ang llama kapag masaya—malambot na kanta na parang sinasabing maganda ang araw.
basic.es: Las llamas tararean cuando están felices, como un amigo peludo que dice ‘hoy la vida está bien.’

edu.en: Llamas spit when annoyed, but they also guard sheep herds from coyotes in farm country.
edu.tl: Dumudura ang llama kapag naiinis, pero pinoprotektahan din nila ang kawan ng tupa mula sa coyote.
edu.es: Las llamas escupen si se molestan, pero también protegen rebaños de coyotes.

habitat.tl: Bundok ng Andes at mataas na pastulan

region.tl: Timog Amerika; pati bukid sa buong mundo

basic.en: Alpaca fleece is softer than sheep wool and comes in 22 natural colors—nature's rainbow yarn!
basic.tl: Mas malambot ang fleece ng alpaca kaysa lana ng tupa at may 22 natural na kulay—rainbow yarn ng kalikasan!
basic.es: ¡La lana de alpaca es más suave que la de oveja y tiene 22 colores naturales!

edu.en: Alpacas are camel relatives with padded feet that tread lightly, reducing damage to mountain grasslands.
edu.tl: Kamag-anak ng kamelyo ang alpaca; may padded na paa na hindi sinisira ang pastulan sa bundok.
edu.es: Las alpacas son parientes de camellos; sus patas acolchadas dañan poco los pastos andinos.

region.en: Peru, Bolivia, and Chile; farms worldwide
region.tl: Peru, Bolivia, Chile; bukid sa buong mundo
region.es: Perú, Bolivia y Chile; granjas mundiales

basic.en: Rabbits cool off through their big ears and can boing three feet high—like a furry pogo stick with a cotton tail.
basic.tl: Nagpapalamig ang kuneho sa malalaking tenga at tumatalon hanggang tatlong talampakan—parang furry pogo stick na may cotton tail.
basic.es: Los conejos se enfrían con orejas grandes y pueden saltar tres pies, como un pogo de peluche con cola de algodón.

edu.en: Rabbits eat their own soft droppings at night to recycle nutrients—a process called coprophagy.
edu.tl: Kinakain ng kuneho ang malambot na dumi sa gabi para muling gamitin ang nutrients—coprophagy.
edu.es: Los conejos comen heces blandas de noche para reciclar nutrientes (coprofagia).

region.tl: Sa buong mundo maliban sa Antarctica

basic.en: Geese fly in a V formation so friends up front block wind for friends behind—teamwork in the sky!
basic.tl: Lumilipad ang gansa sa V formation para harangan ng nasa unahan ang hangin para sa nasa likod—teamwork!
basic.es: ¡Los gansos vuelan en V; los de adelante cortan el viento para los de atrás!

edu.en: Geese mate for life and fiercely guard nests; their honk warns the flock of danger.
edu.tl: Mag-asawa habangbuhay ang gansa at agresibong nagbabantay; ang huni ay babala sa panganib.
edu.es: Los gansos son monógamos y defienden nidos; su graznido alerta al grupo.

region.en: Northern regions; many farm breeds worldwide
region.tl: Hilagang rehiyon; maraming lahi sa buong mundo
region.es: Regiones norteñas; muchas razas en granjas

basic.en: Guinea pigs pop and whistle when excited—they're tiny chat machines with fur!
basic.tl: Umuungol at sumisipol ang guinea pig kapag tuwa—maliit na chat machine na may balahibo!
basic.es: ¡Los conejillos de Indias suenan y silban cuando están felices—pequeñas máquinas de charla!

edu.en: Guinea pigs cannot make their own vitamin C and must get it from fresh veggies like people need oranges.
edu.tl: Hindi kayang gumawa ng vitamin C ang guinea pig—kailangan mula sa sariwang gulay tulad ng tao at orange.
edu.es: Los cuyes no producen vitamina C y la obtienen de verduras frescas.

region.tl: Timog Amerika; sikat na alaga sa buong mundo

basic.en: Hamsters stuff food into cheek pouches that stretch all the way to their shoulders—nature's grocery bags!
basic.tl: Pinupuno ng pagkain ng hamster ang pisngi hanggang balikat—grocery bag ng kalikasan!
basic.es: ¡Los hámsters guardan comida en mochilas de mejilla que llegan al hombro!

edu.en: Hamsters are nocturnal and run miles on wheels at night, storing extra food in burrows.
edu.tl: Gabi ang aktibo ang hamster at tumatakbo ng milya sa wheel; nag-iimbak ng pagkain sa lungga.
edu.es: Los hámsters son nocturnos, corren en ruedas y almacenan comida en madrigueras.

region.en: Syria and Middle East; pets worldwide
region.tl: Syria at Middle East; alaga sa buong mundo
region.es: Siria y Medio Oriente; mascotas mundiales

basic.en: Ferrets do a happy 'war dance'—bouncing and bumping when they're super excited!
basic.tl: Gumagawa ang ferret ng 'war dance'—tumatalon at bumubunggo kapag sobrang tuwa!
basic.es: ¡Los hurones hacen un 'baile de guerra' saltando cuando están muy emocionados!

edu.en: Ferrets have flexible spines that let them chase rabbits through narrow burrows—living tunnel explorers.
edu.tl: Malambot ang gulugod ng ferret para habulin ang kuneho sa makitid na lungga—explorer ng tunnel.
edu.es: Los hurones tienen columna flexible para perseguir conejos en túneles estrechos.

region.tl: Europa; alagang hayop sa buong mundo

basic.en: A mule is a horse-donkey mix with super steady feet, built to climb rocky trails like a mountain helper on four hooves.
basic.tl: Halo ng kabayo at asno ang mule na may sobrang siguradong paa, pang-akyat sa mabatong daan na parang mountain helper na may apat na kuko.
basic.es: La mula es mezcla de caballo y burro con patas súper firmes, hecha para subir senderos rocosos como ayudante de montaña con cuatro pezuñas.

edu.en: Most mules are sterile but inherit a donkey's stamina and a horse's strength for carrying heavy loads.
edu.tl: Karamihan sterile ang mule pero may tibay ng asno at lakas ng kabayo para sa mabibigat na kargamento.
edu.es: La mayoría de mulas son estériles pero combinan resistencia del burro y fuerza del caballo.

region.en: Worldwide where horses and donkeys meet
region.tl: Saan nagkita ang kabayo at asno sa buong mundo
region.es: Donde caballos y burros se cruzan

basic.en: Ponies may be small, but they are tough workers, pulling carts and carrying riders with strong legs that never quit.
basic.tl: Kahit maliit ang pony, matibay itong katuwang—humihila ng kariton at nagdadala ng sakay gamit ang malalakas na paa na hindi sumusuko.
basic.es: Los ponis son pequeños pero fuertes: jalan carretas y llevan jinetes con patas resistentes que no se rinden.

edu.en: Ponies have thicker manes and hardier hooves than horses, adapted to cold windy islands like Shetland.
edu.tl: Mas makapal ang mane at mas matibay ang kuko ng pony kaysa kabayo—sanay sa malamig na isla.
edu.es: Los ponis tienen crines más gruesas y cascos resistentes, adaptados a islas frías.

region.tl: Sa buong mundo; maraming lahi sa isla

basic.en: Pot-bellied pigs love belly rubs and can learn names and tricks fast, proving that “farm cute” can also be super smart.
basic.tl: Mahilig sa belly rub ang pot-bellied pig at mabilis matuto ng pangalan at tricks, patunay na ang “cute” ay puwede ring sobrang talino.
basic.es: Los cerdos vietnamitas adoran las caricias en la panza y aprenden nombres y trucos rápido, demostrando que lo tierno también puede ser muy listo.

edu.en: Originally from Vietnam, these pigs store fat in a sagging belly that helped them survive sparse food seasons.
edu.tl: Mula sa Vietnam; iniimbak ang taba sa lumulubog na tiyan para mabuhay sa panahon ng kakulangan ng pagkain.
edu.es: Originarios de Vietnam, acumulan grasa en la panza para temporadas de escasez.

region.tl: Vietnam; sikat na alaga sa buong mundo

basic.en: Mice whiskers feel the world like radar, helping them zip through dark walls without bumping into trouble.
basic.tl: Parang radar ang whiskers ng daga para dumaan sa madilim na pader nang hindi nabubunggo sa problema.
basic.es: Los bigotes del ratón sienten el mundo como radar y lo ayudan a pasar por paredes oscuras sin chocar.

edu.en: A mouse heart beats about 600 times per minute—that's ten beats every second!
edu.tl: Tumitibok ang puso ng daga ng ~600 beses bawat minuto—sampung beses bawat segundo!
edu.es: El corazón de un ratón late unas 600 veces por minuto—¡diez latidos por segundo!

region.tl: Sa buong mundo maliban sa Antarctica

basic.en: Rats giggle when tickled—scientists hear it with special microphones!
basic.tl: Tumatawa ang daga kapag kinikiliti—maririnig ng siyentipiko gamit ang espesyal na mikropono!
basic.es: ¡Las ratas se ríen cuando las hacen cosquillas—se oye con micrófonos especiales!

edu.en: Rats are excellent swimmers and can tread water for three days; their tails help with balance.
edu.tl: Mahusay lumangoy ang daga at kaya ang tubig ng 3 araw; tumutulong ang buntot sa balance.
edu.es: Las ratas nadan bien y pueden flotar tres días; la cola ayuda al equilibrio.

diet.es: Granos, fruta y restos de carne (omnívoro)

heightLength.tl: Mga 6–12 in ang haba (depende sa laki ng tank)

basic.en: Goldfish remember feeding tricks for months, busting the myth that they forget everything in three seconds flat.
basic.tl: Natatandaan ng goldfish ang tricks sa pagkain nang buwan-buwan—hindi totoo ang three-second memory myth.
basic.es: Los peces dorados recuerdan trucos por meses, rompiendo el mito de que olvidan en tres segundos.

edu.en: Goldfish see more colors than humans and grow to match their tank if given clean water and space.
edu.tl: Mas maraming kulay ang nakikita ng goldfish kaysa tao at lumalaki ayon sa espasyo at malinis na tubig.
edu.es: Los goldfish ven más colores que nosotros y crecen según el espacio y agua limpia.

region.tl: Asya originally; alaga sa buong mundo

diet.en: Fish flakes, plants, and small bugs (omnivore)
diet.tl: Fish flakes, halaman, at maliliit na insekto (omniboro)

basic.en: When scared, hedgehogs roll into a spiky ball—like a living pin cushion!
basic.tl: Kapag natatakot, nagiging bola ang hedgehog na may tinik—parang living pin cushion!
basic.es: ¡Cuando se asustan, los erizos se enrollan en una bola espinosaa!

edu.en: Hedgehogs have about 5,000 spines made of keratin—the same protein as your fingernails.
edu.tl: May ~5,000 tinik ang hedgehog na gawa sa keratin—pareho ng kuko ng tao.
edu.es: Los erizos tienen unas 5,000 púas de queratina, como las uñas.

basic.en: Wild boar piglets have striped coats like tiny tigers—they blend into forest shadows!
basic.tl: May guhit-guhit ang biik ng wild boar parang maliit na tigre—nagkakamuflage sa gubat!
basic.es: ¡Los jabalíes bebés tienen rayas como mini tigres para camuflarse!

edu.en: Wild boars use tough snouts to uproot roots and can run up to 30 mph when escaping danger.
edu.tl: Inuukit ng wild boar ang ugat gamit ang matibay na ilong at tumatakbo hanggang 30 mph kapag tumatakas.
edu.es: Los jabalíes arrancan raíces con el hocico y corren hasta 30 mph al huir.

region.es: Europa, Asia y norte de África

diet.tl: Ugat, mani, at maliliit na hayop (omniboro)

basic.en: Opossums play dead so convincingly that predators walk away—ultimate pretend nap!
basic.tl: Nagpapanggap na patay ang opossum nang totoo-talaga—pinakamahusay na pretend nap!
basic.es: ¡Los oposums fingen estar muertos tan bien que los depredadores se van!

edu.en: Opossums eat ticks by the thousands, helping reduce Lyme disease in some areas.
edu.tl: Kumakain ang opossum ng libo-libong tick, tumutulong bawasan ang Lyme disease.
edu.es: Los oposums comen miles de garrapatas, reduciendo enfermedad de Lyme.

basic.en: Sugar gliders glide through trees like furry kites using skin flaps between their legs!
basic.tl: Lumilipad ang sugar glider sa puno parang furry kite gamit ang balat sa pagitan ng binti!
basic.es: ¡Los petauros planean entre árboles como cometas peludas!

edu.en: These marsupials carry babies in a pouch and use big eyes for night hunting of sap and insects.
edu.tl: May pouch ang marsupial na ito para sa sanggol; malaking mata para manghuli ng katas at insekto gabi.
edu.es: Estos marsupiales llevan crías en bolsa y cazan savia e insectos de noche.

basic.en: Vampire bats share blood meals with hungry roost-mates—real-life food sharing friends!
basic.tl: Nagbabahagi ang vampire bat ng dugo sa gutom na kasama—tunay na food sharing friends!
basic.es: ¡Los murciélagos vampiro comparten sangre con compañeros hambrientos!

edu.en: Their saliva has a chemical that keeps blood flowing; they drink only a tablespoon without harming hosts.
edu.tl: May kemikal sa laway para dumaloy ang dugo; umiinom lang ng kutsara nang hindi pinapatay ang host.
edu.es: Su saliva evita que la sangre coagule; beben solo una cucharada sin matar al huésped.

diet.tl: Dugo mula sa baka at ibon (karnivoro)

basic.en: Black bears climb trees like giant teddy bears looking for honey!
basic.tl: Umaakyat sa puno ang black bear parang malaking teddy bear na naghahanap ng honey!
basic.es: ¡Los osos negros trepan árboles como ositos buscando miel!

edu.en: They have an excellent sense of smell—seven times stronger than a dog's.
edu.tl: Mahusay ang pang-amoy—pitong beses na mas malakas kaysa aso.
edu.es: Su olfato es siete veces más fuerte que el de un perro.

basic.en: Brown bears catch salmon leaping up waterfalls—nature's ultimate fishing show!
basic.tl: Hinuhuli ng brown bear ang salmon na tumatalon sa talon—pinakamagandang fishing show!
basic.es: ¡Los osos pardos atrapan salmones saltando cascadas!

edu.en: Grizzlies dig cozy dens and sleep through winter without eating for months.
edu.tl: Humuhukay ang grizzly ng lungga at natutulog buong taglamig nang walang kain.
edu.es: Los grizzlies hibernan meses sin comer en madrigueras.

basic.en: When startled, they flash white tail flags like built-in warning signals!
basic.tl: Kapag gulat, ibinubuka ang puting buntot parang warning signal!
basic.es: ¡Muestran la cola blanca como señal de alarma!

edu.en: Only males grow antlers that fall off and regrow bigger each year.
edu.tl: Ang lalaki lang ang may sungay na nalalagas at tumutubo ulit taun-taon.
edu.es: Solo los machos tienen astas que se renuevan cada año.

basic.en: Foxes use Earth's magnetic field to aim pounces through snow at hidden mice!
basic.tl: Ginagamit ng fox ang magnetic field ng Earth para tumalon sa niyebe at habulin ang daga!
basic.es: ¡Los zorros usan el campo magnético para cazar bajo la nieve!

edu.en: Their bushy tails keep them warm and help balance during sharp turns.
edu.tl: Pinapainit ng mabalahibong buntot at tumutulong sa balance sa matalim na liko.
edu.es: La cola esponjosa calienta y ayuda a equilibrar en giros.

basic.en: Raccoons wash food in water—even when there's no water, they still 'wash'!
basic.tl: Hinuhugasan ng raccoon ang pagkain sa tubig—kahit walang tubig, 'hugas' pa rin!
basic.es: ¡Los mapaches lavan comida en agua, a veces sin agua!

edu.en: Their front paws have touch receptors like tiny hands for feeling food in dark.
edu.tl: May touch receptors ang kamay na parang maliit na kamay para maramdaman ang pagkain sa dilim.
edu.es: Sus patas delanteras sienten comida como manos en la oscuridad.

basic.en: Squirrels pretend to bury nuts to trick thieves—squirrel spy games!
basic.tl: Nagpapanggap ang squirrel na nagtatanim ng mani para lokohin ang magnanakaw!
basic.es: ¡Las ardillas fingen enterrar nueces para engañar ladrones!

edu.en: They spread oak forests by forgetting buried acorns that sprout into trees.
edu.tl: Pinapalaganap ang gubat ng oak dahil sa nakakalimutang acorn na tumutubo.
edu.es: Esparren bosques de roble al olvidar bellotas enterradas.

region.tl: Buong mundo maliban sa Australia at Antarctica

basic.en: Moose antlers can span 6 ft—wider than most cars!
basic.tl: Maaaring 6 ft ang lapad ng sungay ng moose—mas malapad kaysa maraming kotse!
basic.es: ¡Las astas del alce pueden medir 6 pies, más anchas que un coche!

edu.en: They are excellent swimmers and eat underwater plants from northern lakes.
edu.tl: Mahusay lumangoy at kumakain ng halaman sa ilalim ng tubig sa hilagang lawa.
edu.es: Nadan bien y comen plantas subacuáticas en lagos del norte.

basic.en: Male elk bugle in fall—a wild trumpet call that echoes through mountains!
basic.tl: Umuungol ang lalaking elk sa taglagas—parang trumpeta sa bundok!
basic.es: ¡Los alces machos braman en otoño como trompetas en montañas!

edu.en: Elk migrate following snow lines to find fresh grass in high meadows.
edu.tl: Naglalakbay ang elk kasunod ng niyebe para hanapin ang sariwang damo sa mataas na parang.
edu.es: Migran siguiendo la nieve hacia praderas altas.

diet.tl: Damo, balat ng puno, at dahon (herbivoro)

basic.en: Beavers build dams that create ponds—animal engineers changing whole valleys!
basic.tl: Gumagawa ang beaver ng dam na gumagawa ng pond—animal engineers na binabago ang lambak!
basic.es: ¡Los castores construyen presas que crean lagunas!

edu.en: Their orange teeth never stop growing; gnawing wood keeps them trimmed.
edu.tl: Hindi tumitigil lumaki ang orange na ngipin; ang pagngatngat ng kahoy ang pumuputol.
edu.es: Sus dientes naranjas crecen siempre; morder madera los desgasta.

diet.tl: Balat ng puno at halaman sa tubig (herbivoro)

basic.en: Porcupines have 30,000 quills—each one a tiny spiky umbrella for protection!
basic.tl: May 30,000 tinik ang porcupine—bawat isa parang maliit na spiky umbrella!
basic.es: ¡Los puercoespines tienen 30,000 púas como paraguas espinosos!

edu.en: Quills have barbs that loosen when touched, deterring predators without a fight.
edu.tl: May barb ang tinik na lumalabas kapag hinawakan—nakakatakot sa predator.
edu.es: Las púas tienen púas que se sueltan al tacto, disuadiendo depredadores.

basic.en: Skunks warn with stomps and tail raises before spraying stinky defense juice!
basic.tl: Babala muna ang skunk sa pagtapak at pagtaas ng buntot bago mag-spray ng mabahong depensa!
basic.es: ¡Las mofetas avisan pisando y levantando la cola antes de rociar!

edu.en: Their spray can hit targets 10 ft away and linger for days.
edu.tl: Maaaring abutin ng spray ang 10 ft at tumagal ng araw.
edu.es: El rocío alcanza 10 pies y dura días.

basic.en: Badgers dig tunnel homes with bedrooms and pantries—underground mansions!
basic.tl: Humuhukay ang badger ng tunnel na may kwarto at pantry—underground mansion!
basic.es: ¡Los tejones excavan túneles con dormitorios y despensa!

edu.en: Powerful claws can dig faster than a person with a shovel in soft soil.
edu.tl: Mas mabilis humukay ang matibay na kuko kaysa tao na may pala.
edu.es: Sus garras cavan más rápido que una pala en tierra blanda.

basic.en: Chipmunks stuff cheeks with nuts until they look like furry footballs!
basic.tl: Pinupuno ng mani ang pisngi ng chipmunk hanggang mukhang furry football!
basic.es: ¡Las ardillas listadas llenan mejillas hasta parecer pelotas!

edu.en: One chipmunk can gather thousands of nuts in autumn for winter pantries.
edu.tl: Maaaring mangolekta ng libo-libong mani ang isang chipmunk sa taglagas.
edu.es: Una ardilla puede acumular miles de nueces en otoño.

basic.en: Moles are champion diggers—they can tunnel 18 ft in one hour underground!
basic.tl: Kampeon humukay ang mole—18 ft sa isang oras sa ilalim ng lupa!
basic.es: ¡Los topos excavan 18 pies en una hora bajo tierra!

edu.en: They eat their weight in worms daily using a star-shaped nose to feel prey.
edu.tl: Kumakain ng worms na kasing-timbang araw-araw gamit ang star-shaped na ilong.
edu.es: Comen su peso en gusanos al día con nariz estrellada sensorial.

basic.en: Stoats do a hopping dance to hypnotize rabbits before a chase!
basic.tl: Sumasayaw ang stoat na tumatalon para hipnotismo ang kuneho bago habulin!
basic.es: ¡Los armiños bailan saltando para confundir conejos!

edu.en: In winter many turn white as ermine, hiding in snow from hawks.
edu.tl: Sa taglamig puti ang marami parang ermine, nagtatago sa niyebe mula sa lawin.
edu.es: En invierno muchos se vuelven blancos para camuflarse en nieve.

basic.en: Ermine wear a snowy white coat in winter with a black-tipped tail like a paintbrush!
basic.tl: Puting amerikana sa taglamig ang ermine na may itim na dulo ng buntot parang paintbrush!
basic.es: ¡El armiño blanco invernal tiene cola negra como pincel!

edu.en: Royal robes in history used ermine fur; the animal sheds white coat in spring.
edu.tl: Ginamit ang balahibo ng ermine sa royal robes; nagbabago ng kulay sa tagsibol.
edu.es: La piel real usaba armiño; muda a marrón en primavera.

basic.en: Wolverines are small but fearless—they chase bears away from food!
basic.tl: Maliit pero matapang ang wolverine—pinapalayas ang oso mula sa pagkain!
basic.es: ¡Los glotones son pequeños pero ahuyentan osos de su comida!

edu.en: Huge paws act like snowshoes in deep powder across northern wildlands.
edu.tl: Parang snowshoe ang malaking paa sa malalim na niyebe sa hilaga.
edu.es: Sus patas grandes funcionan como raquetas en nieve profunda.

region.en: Northern North America, Europe, and Asia
region.tl: Hilagang Hilagang Amerika, Europa, at Asya
region.es: Norte de Norteamérica, Europa y Asia

basic.en: Woodpeckers drum on trees 20 times a second without headaches—special shock-proof skulls!
basic.tl: 20 beses bawat segundo ang tukmol ng woodpecker nang walang sakit ng ulo—shock-proof na bungo!
basic.es: ¡Picotean 20 veces por segundo con cráneos amortiguados!

edu.en: Long tongues wrap around brain case and shoot out to grab insects in holes.
edu.tl: Pabilog ang dila sa bungo at lumalabas para kunin ang insekto sa butas.
edu.es: La lengua larga envuelve el cráneo y atrapa insectos en huecos.

region.tl: Buong mundo maliban sa Australia at Antarctica

basic.en: Hawks see eight times sharper than humans—they spot a mouse from a skyscraper height!
basic.tl: 8 beses na mas matalas ang paningin kaysa tao—nakikita ang daga mula sa taas ng skyscraper!
basic.es: ¡Ven ocho veces mejor que nosotros y detectan ratones desde rascacielos!

edu.en: Sharp talons and curved beaks are built for catching prey mid-air or on ground.
edu.tl: Matutulis ang talon at baluktot ang tuka para habulin ang biktima sa hangin o lupa.
edu.es: Garras y pico curvo atrapan presas en aire o suelo.

basic.en: Peregrine falcons dive faster than race cars—over 200 mph!
basic.tl: Mas mabilis ang dive ng peregrine falcon kaysa race car—200+ mph!
basic.es: ¡El halcón peregrino se lanza a más de 200 mph!

edu.en: A bony ridge in the nostril slows air during dives so they can breathe.
edu.tl: May buto sa butas ng ilong para pabagalin ang hangin habang dive at makahinga.
edu.es: Una cresta nasal frena el aire en picados para respirar.

basic.en: The cacomistle tiptoes on branches at night, using its ringed tail like a balance rope in a fuzzy circus act.
basic.tl: Sa gabi, dahan-dahang naglalakad ang cacomistle sa mga sanga, gamit ang buntot na may singsing-singsing parang lubid sa isang malambot na circus act.
basic.es: El cacomistle camina de puntillas por las ramas de noche y usa su cola anillada como cuerda de equilibrio en un circo peludo.

edu.en: Big eyes and ankles that rotate help them climb down trees headfirst.
edu.tl: Malaking mata at rotating ankle tumutulong bumaba ng puno nang ulo muna.
edu.es: Ojos grandes y tobillos giratorios bajan árboles de cabeza.

basic.en: A genet is a night-time tree acrobat, sliding and leaping with a long striped tail that looks like a sneaky balance pole.
basic.tl: Night-time acrobat sa puno ang genet, dumudulas at tumatalon na may mahabang buntot na guhit-guhit parang pang-balanse na patago.
basic.es: La gineta es acróbata nocturna de los árboles, saltando con una cola larga rayada que parece una vara de equilibrio.

edu.en: They hunt at night using whiskers and hearing in African woodlands.
edu.tl: Gabi manghuli gamit ang whiskers at pandinig sa gubat ng Aprika.
edu.es: Cazan de noche con bigotes y oído agudo en bosques africanos.

basic.en: Civets smell like popcorn from scent glands—stinky but surprisingly snacky!
basic.tl: Amoy popcorn ang civet mula sa scent gland—mabaho pero parang meryenda!
basic.es: ¡Las civetas huelen a palomitas por glándulas odoríferas!

edu.en: They help spread seeds through forests after eating ripe fruit.
edu.tl: Tumutulong magkalat ng buto sa gubat pagkatapos kumain ng hinog na prutas.
edu.es: Dispersan semillas al comer fruta madura.

basic.en: Gorillas beat their chests to show strength—but they also giggle when tickled!
basic.tl: Binubugbog ng gorilla ang dibdib para ipakita ang lakas—pero tumatawa rin kapag kinikiliti!
basic.es: ¡Los gorilas golpean el pecho pero se ríen si les hacen cosquillas!

edu.en: They live in close family troops led by a silverback who protects everyone.
edu.tl: Nabubuhay sa pamilya na pinamumunuan ng silverback na nagpoprotekta.
edu.es: Viven en familias lideradas por un espalda plateada protector.

basic.en: Chimps use sticks as tools to fish termites—wild scientists with snacks!
basic.tl: Gumagamit ang chimp ng stick para manghuli ng termite—wild scientists na may meryenda!
basic.es: ¡Usan palos para pescar termitas, científicos salvajes!

edu.en: They share 98% of our DNA and learn skills by watching friends.
edu.tl: 98% ng DNA natin ang pareho at natututo sa pamamagitan ng panonood sa kaibigan.
edu.es: Comparten 98% de nuestro ADN y aprenden observando.

basic.en: Orangutans build fresh leaf nests to sleep in, like forest architects making a new bed in the treetops each night.
basic.tl: Gumagawa ang orangutan ng bagong pugad na dahon para tulugan, parang forest architect na gumagawa ng bagong kama sa tuktok ng puno gabi-gabi.
basic.es: Los orangutanes construyen nidos nuevos de hojas para dormir, como arquitectos del bosque que hacen una cama en las copas cada noche.

edu.en: They build new leafy nests in trees every single night to sleep safely.
edu.tl: Gumagawa ng bagong nest na dahon sa puno bawat gabi para ligtas matulog.
edu.es: Construyen nidos de hojas nuevos cada noche en árboles.

basic.en: Sloths move so slowly that algae grows on their fur—walking green gardens!
basic.tl: Mabagal ang sloth kaya tumutubo ang algae sa balahibo—walking green garden!
basic.es: ¡Se mueven tan lento que algas crecen en su pelaje!

edu.en: They descend trees once a week to poop, risking predators to fertilize roots.
edu.tl: Bumababa linggu-linggo para dumumi at patabain ang ugat, delikado pero mahalaga.
edu.es: Bajan una vez por semana a defecar, arriesgándose para fertilizar raíces.

basic.en: Howler monkeys have the loudest call of any land animal—it can be heard 3 miles away!
basic.tl: Pinakamalakas ang tunog ng howler monkey—maririnig sa 3 milya!
basic.es: ¡Su aullido se oye a 3 millas, el más fuerte en tierra!

edu.en: A special throat bone acts like an amplifier for their dawn chorus.
edu.tl: Espesyal na buto sa lalamunan parang amplifier sa umagang koro.
edu.es: Un hueso en la garganta amplifica su coro al amanecer.

basic.en: Capuchins use rocks to crack nuts—little monkeys with stone hammers!
basic.tl: Gumagamit ang capuchin ng bato para basagin ang mani—maliit na unggoy na may stone hammer!
basic.es: ¡Usan piedras para romper nueces, martillos de piedra!

edu.en: Smart and social, they groom friends to build trust in troops.
edu.tl: Matalino at social; nag-aayos ng balahibo ng kaibigan para magtiwala.
edu.es: Inteligentes y sociales, se acicalan para crear confianza.

diet.tl: Prutas, insekto, at maliliit na hayop (omniboro)

heightLength.en: About 2–3 ft long; tail longer than body
heightLength.tl: Mga 2–3 ft; mas mahaba ang buntot kaysa katawan
heightLength.es: Alrededor de 2–3 pies; cola más larga que el cuerpo

basic.en: Spider monkeys swing by tails like fifth hands—acrobats of the jungle!
basic.tl: Umuugoy ang spider monkey sa buntot parang ikalimang kamay—acrobat ng gubat!
basic.es: ¡Se balancean con la cola como quinta mano, acróbatas!

edu.en: Prehensile tails grip branches so they can reach far for fruit.
edu.tl: Humahawak ang prehensile tail sa sanga para abot ang prutas.
edu.es: Colas prensiles agarran ramas para alcanzar fruta.

basic.en: Ring-tailed lemurs sunbathe sitting yoga-style with arms out—warming up together!
basic.tl: Naka-yoga ang ring-tailed lemur sa araw na nakabukas ang braso—sabay nagpapainit!
basic.es: ¡Lémures de cola anillada toman sol en pose de yoga!

edu.en: Madagascar lemurs are found nowhere else on Earth—they're island originals.
edu.tl: Sa Madagascar lang matatagpuan ang lemur—island originals!
edu.es: Los lémures solo viven en Madagascar, únicos de la isla.

basic.en: A tapir looks like a pig-elephant mix with a mini trunk, perfect for vacuuming up leaves and fruit on the forest floor.
basic.tl: Mukhang halo ng baboy at elepante ang tapir na may mini trunk, bagay na bagay sa pag-“vacuum” ng dahon at prutas sa sahig ng gubat.
basic.es: El tapir parece mezcla de cerdo y elefante con trompa pequeña, perfecta para “aspirar” hojas y fruta del suelo del bosque.

edu.en: They are living fossils that have changed little for millions of years.
edu.tl: Living fossil na halos hindi nagbago sa milyon-milyong taon.
edu.es: Fósiles vivientes casi iguales por millones de años.

diet.tl: Dahon, prutas, at halaman sa tubig (herbivoro)

basic.en: Okapis have zebra stripes on legs but are giraffe relatives—forest giraffes in disguise!
basic.tl: May guhit ng zebra ang binti ng okapi pero kamag-anak ng giraffe—forest giraffe na naka disguise!
basic.es: ¡Rayas de cebra en patas pero parientes de jirafas!

edu.en: Long tongues clean ears and strip leaves in Congo rainforest shadows.
edu.tl: Mahaba ang dila para linisin ang tainga at dahon sa dilim ng Congo.
edu.es: Lengua larga limpia orejas y arranca hojas en Congo.

basic.en: Chameleons shoot tongues faster than you blink to catch bugs!
basic.tl: Pinuputok ng chameleon ang dila nang mas mabilis kaysa ikim blink para habulin ang insekto!
basic.es: ¡Disparan la lengua más rápido que un parpadeo!

edu.en: Special skin cells shift color for mood, temperature, and camouflage.
edu.tl: Nagbabago ang kulay ng balat para sa mood, init, at camouflage.
edu.es: Células de piel cambian color por humor, temperatura y camuflaje.

basic.en: Green iguanas can drop their tails when grabbed—it wiggles while they escape!
basic.tl: Napuputol ang buntot ng green iguana kapag hinawakan—kumikislap habang tumatakas!
basic.es: ¡Suelta la cola si la agarran; se retuerce mientras huye!

edu.en: They bask in sun to warm up, then dive into rivers when scared.
edu.tl: Nagpapainit sa araw tapos tumatalon sa ilog kapag takot.
edu.es: Toman sol para calentarse y saltan a ríos al asustarse.

basic.en: Boas squeeze prey gently with muscles, then swallow whole—no chewing needed!
basic.tl: Pinipisil ng boa ang biktima tapos nilalamon buo—walang ngatngat!
basic.es: ¡Aprietan presas y las tragan enteras sin masticar!

edu.en: Heat-sensing pits on lips detect warm bodies in darkness.
edu.tl: May heat-sensing pit sa labi para maramdaman ang init ng biktima sa dilim.
edu.es: Fosas térmicas en labios detectan calor en la oscuridad.

basic.en: Pythons have stretchy jaws that open wide enough to swallow deer!
basic.tl: Kayang bumuka nang malapad ang panga ng python para lamunin ang usa!
basic.es: ¡Mandíbulas elásticas tragan ciervos enteros!

edu.en: They coil around eggs to keep them warm—snake mom incubators!
edu.tl: Pinaliligiran ng python ang itlog para painitin—snake mom incubator!
edu.es: Enroscan huevos para incubarlos como mamás serpiente.

basic.en: Tiny poison dart frogs wear neon colors that shout 'Don't eat me!'
basic.tl: Maliit na poison dart frog na may neon na kulay—'Wag akong kainin!'
basic.es: ¡Minúsculas y neón advierten '¡No me comas!'

edu.en: Their poison comes from ants they eat; captive frogs without ants are harmless.
edu.tl: Galing sa langgam ang lason; harmless ang frog sa captivity kung walang langgam.
edu.es: El veneno viene de hormigas; en cautiverio sin hormigas son inofensivas.

basic.en: When scared they flash giant red eyes and orange feet—surprise superhero reveal!
basic.tl: Kapag takot, ibinubukas ang malalaking pulang mata at orange na paa—superhero reveal!
basic.es: ¡Muestran ojos rojos y pies naranjas al asustarse!

edu.en: Sticky toe pads let them cling to wet leaves during rain.
edu.tl: Malagkit ang daliri para kumapit sa basang dahon sa ulan.
edu.es: Dedos pegajosos se agarran a hojas mojadas.

habitat.tl: Puno malapit sa tubig sa rainforest
habitat.es: Árboles de selva cerca del agua

basic.en: Giant anteaters have 2-ft tongues that flick in and out 150 times a minute!
basic.tl: 2-ft na dila ng giant anteater na pumapasok-labas 150 beses bawat minuto!
basic.es: ¡Lengua de 2 pies entra y sale 150 veces por minuto!

edu.en: Curved claws tear open termite mounds but fold away when walking.
edu.tl: Baluktot na kuko ang bumubukas ng termite mound pero tinatago kapag naglalakad.
edu.es: Garras curvas abren termiteros y se repliegan al caminar.

basic.en: Armadillos jump straight up when startled—surprise bounce armor!
basic.tl: Tumatalon pataas ang armadillo kapag gulat—surprise bounce armor!
basic.es: ¡Saltan verticalmente al asustarse con armadura!

edu.en: Bony plates cover them; three-banded types roll into tight balls.
edu.tl: May butong plate; ang three-banded ay nagiging bola.
edu.es: Placas óseas los cubren; algunos ruedan en bola.

basic.en: Kinkajous hang upside down to eat honey—they're rainforest honey bears!
basic.tl: Nakabitin ang kinkajou para kumain ng honey—rainforest honey bear!
basic.es: ¡Cuelgan boca abajo comiendo miel, ositos de miel!

edu.en: Prehensile tails and night vision help them raid beehives in treetops.
edu.tl: Prehensile tail at night vision tumutulong magnakaw ng honey sa puno.
edu.es: Cola prensil y visión nocturna saquean colmenas.

basic.en: Aye-ayes tap trees with a long middle finger to find grubs—woodpecker fingers!
basic.tl: Tumutuk-tok ang aye-aye sa puno gamit ang mahabang gitnang daliri—woodpecker fingers!
basic.es: ¡Golpetean árboles con dedo medio largo como carpinteros!

edu.en: That same finger scoops insects from tunnels after echolocating them.
edu.tl: Pinapasok ang daliri sa tunnel pagkatapos mahanap ang insekto.
edu.es: El dedo saca insectos de túneles tras localizarlos.

basic.en: Tarsiers have eyes bigger than their brain—they see perfectly in the dark!
basic.tl: Mas malaki ang mata kaysa utak ng tarsier—perfect sa dilim!
basic.es: ¡Ojos más grandes que el cerebro, visión nocturna perfecta!

edu.en: They leap between trees catching insects mid-air with tiny hands.
edu.tl: Tumatalon sa puno habang hinuhuli ang insekto sa hangin.
edu.es: Saltan entre árboles atrapando insectos en el aire.

basic.en: Fruit bats spread seeds and pollen as they fly, helping new trees grow—like night gardeners with wings.
basic.tl: Habang lumilipad, nagkakalat ang fruit bat ng buto at pollen para tumubo ang bagong puno—parang night gardener na may pakpak.
basic.es: Los murciélagos frugívoros esparcen semillas y polen al volar, ayudando a crecer árboles nuevos, como jardineros nocturnos con alas.

edu.en: Large eyes see at dusk; they use smell more than echolocation.
edu.tl: Malaking mata sa takipsilim; mas umaasa sa amoy kaysa echolocation.
edu.es: Ojos grandes al anochecer; olfato más que ecolocalización.

basic.en: Pangolins roll into scaly balls—real-life dragon eggs with legs!
basic.tl: Nagiging bola ang pangolin na may kaliskis—dragon egg na may paa!
basic.es: ¡Se enrollan en bolas escamosas como huevos de dragón!

edu.en: One pangolin can eat 70 million ants a year with a sticky tongue.
edu.tl: Kaya kumain ng 70 milyong langgam taun-taon gamit ang malagkit na dila.
edu.es: Comen 70 millones de hormigas al año con lengua pegajosa.

basic.en: Bush babies leap 6 ft between branches with huge eyes like night goggles!
basic.tl: Tumatalon ng 6 ft ang bush baby sa sanga na may malaking mata parang night goggles!
basic.es: ¡Saltan 6 pies entre ramas con ojos como visión nocturna!

edu.en: They mark paths with urine so they can jump safely in the dark.
edu.tl: Minamarkahan ang daan ng ihi para ligtas tumalon sa dilim.
edu.es: Marcan rutas con orina para saltar seguros de noche.

basic.en: Green anacondas are the heaviest snakes—they swim like silent river logs!
basic.tl: Pinakamabigat ang green anaconda—lumalangoy parang tahimik na troso!
basic.es: ¡Las anacondas verdes son las serpientes más pesadas, nadan como troncos!

edu.en: Females are larger and sometimes eat caiman in swamp ambushes.
edu.tl: Mas malaki ang babae at minsan kumakain ng caiman sa swamp.
edu.es: Las hembras son más grandes y comen caimanes en pantanos.

basic.en: Koalas hug eucalyptus trees and nap up to 20 hours, saving energy like fluffy little phone chargers that sleep most of the day.
basic.tl: Yakap ng koala ang eucalyptus at natutulog hanggang 20 oras, nag-iipon ng lakas na parang fluffy charger na halos buong araw ay tulog.
basic.es: Los koalas abrazan eucaliptos y duermen hasta 20 horas, ahorrando energía como cargadores peludos que pasan casi todo el día dormidos.

edu.en: Special bacteria in their gut digest toxic eucalyptus leaves other animals can't eat.
edu.tl: Espesyal na bacteria sa tiyan ang tumutulong sa toxic na dahon ng eucalyptus.
edu.es: Bacterias en el intestino digieren hojas de eucalipto tóxicas.

basic.en: Wombats poop cubes so poop doesn't roll away on hills—nature's dice!
basic.tl: Kubo ang dumi ng wombat para hindi gumulong sa burol—nature's dice!
basic.es: ¡Los wombats hacen heces cúbicas que no ruedan cuesta abajo!

edu.en: Their rear ends are armored plates that block burrow entrances from predators.
edu.tl: May armored plate ang puwitan para harangan ang lungga mula sa predator.
edu.es: Su trasero acorazado bloquea madrigueras de depredadores.

basic.en: Tasmanian devils scream and sneeze to talk—it sounds wild but it's just chat!
basic.tl: Sumisigaw at umuubo ang Tasmanian devil para mag-usap—wild pero chat lang!
basic.es: ¡Gritan y estornudan para comunicarse, suena fiero pero es charla!

edu.en: Strong jaws can crunch bone; they eat carrion and help clean the forest.
edu.tl: Kayang durugin ng malakas na ngipin ang buto; kumakain ng bangkay at nililinis ang gubat.
edu.es: Mandíbulas fuertes trituran huesos; comen carroña limpiando bosques.

heightLength.en: About 4 ft at shoulder; 6–8 ft long
heightLength.tl: Mga 4 ft sa balikat; 6–8 ft haba
heightLength.es: Alrededor de 4 pies al hombro; 6–8 pies de largo

basic.en: A lion's roar can be heard 5 miles away—it shakes the savannah!
basic.tl: Maririnig ang ungal ng leon sa 5 milya—yanig ang savannah!
basic.es: ¡El rugido del león se oye a 5 millas y sacude la sabana!

edu.en: Only about one in eight hunts succeeds; teamwork makes pride meals possible.
edu.tl: Isa sa walong pangangaso lang ang tagumpay; teamwork ng pride ang susi.
edu.es: Solo uno de cada ocho cacerías triunfa; la manada coopera.

region.tl: Sub-Saharan Aprika; maliit na populasyon sa Asya
region.es: África subsahariana; pequeña población en Asia

basic.en: An elephant’s trunk has thousands of muscles, so it can pick up a peanut, spray a shower, or give a gentle high-five.
basic.tl: Libu-libong muscle ang trunk ng elepante—kayang pulutin ang peanut, mag-shower, o mag-high-five nang dahan-dahan.
basic.es: La trompa del elefante tiene miles de músculos: puede tomar un maní, darse una ducha o dar un choque de mano suave.

edu.en: They mourn dead herd members and remember water sources for decades.
edu.tl: Nagdadalamhati at natatandaan ang bukal ng tubig sa loob ng dekada.
edu.es: Lloran a muertos y recuerdan fuentes de agua por décadas.

diet.tl: Damo, balat ng puno, at prutas (herbivoro)

basic.en: Hyena giggles sound spooky but mean they're excited during a meal!
basic.tl: Nakakatakot ang tawa ng hyena pero ibig sabihin tuwa habang kumakain!
basic.es: ¡Sus 'risas' suenan raras pero muestran emoción al comer!

edu.en: Powerful jaws crush bone to get marrow; females lead clans.
edu.tl: Kayang durugin ng ngipin ang buto para sa marrow; babae ang lider.
edu.es: Mandíbulas trituran huesos; las hembras lideran el clan.

basic.en: Aardvarks slurp ants with a sticky tongue longer than a ruler, turning anthills into all-you-can-eat buffets.
basic.tl: Sinisipsip ng aardvark ang langgam gamit ang malagkit na dila na mas mahaba pa sa ruler—buffet ng anthill.
basic.es: Los osos hormigueros lamen hormigas con lengua pegajosa más larga que una regla: buffet de hormiguero.

edu.en: They dig new burrows nightly and leave old ones for other animals to use.
edu.tl: Gumagawa ng bagong lungga gabi-gabi; iniwan ang luma para sa ibang hayop.
edu.es: Cavan madrigueras nuevas cada noche; las viejas sirven a otros animales.

heightLength.tl: Mga 1 ft ang taas; 1.5 ft haba
heightLength.es: Alrededor de 1 pie de alto; 1.5 pies de largo

basic.en: Fennec foxes have giant ears that radiate heat and hear beetles under sand!
basic.tl: Malalaking tainga ang fennec fox para magpalamig at marinig ang beetle sa buhangin!
basic.es: ¡Orejas gigantes disipan calor y oyen escarabajos bajo arena!

edu.en: Nocturnal hunters survive deserts without drinking much water from food.
edu.tl: Gabi manghuli at nabubuhay sa disyerto nang kaunting inom mula sa pagkain.
edu.es: Cazan de noche y obtienen agua de su comida.

basic.en: Jerboas bounce on kangaroo legs and barely use front paws—desert pogo sticks!
basic.tl: Tumatalon ang jerboa sa kangaroo legs—desert pogo stick!
basic.es: ¡Saltan como canguros del desierto en patas largas!

edu.en: Long tail helps balance during hops up to 10 ft in a single leap.
edu.tl: Tumutulong ang mahabang buntot sa balance sa 10 ft na talon.
edu.es: Cola larga equilibra saltos de hasta 10 pies.

basic.en: Scorpions glow green under UV light—secret disco armor!
basic.tl: Kumikinang berde ang scorpion sa UV light—secret disco armor!
basic.es: ¡Brillan verdes con luz UV, armadura disco secreta!

edu.en: They've existed over 400 million years; mothers carry babies on their backs.
edu.tl: 400 milyong taon na; dinadala ng ina ang sanggol sa likod.
edu.es: Existen 400 millones de años; madres cargan crías.

region.tl: Mainit na rehiyon sa buong mundo

basic.en: Geckos stick to ceilings with toe pads so sticky they can hold your weight scaled down!
basic.tl: Kumakapit ang gecko sa kisame—sobrang sticky ang toe pads!
basic.es: ¡Se pegan al techo con dedos adhesivos increíbles!

edu.en: Some species chirp and many drop tails to escape predators.
edu.tl: May species na humihiyaw at napuputol ang buntot para tumakas.
edu.es: Algunas pían y sueltan cola al escapar.

region.tl: Mainit na rehiyon sa buong mundo

basic.en: Polar bears have black skin under white fur—sun heats them like solar panels!
basic.tl: Itim ang balat sa ilalim ng puting balahibo—parang solar panel sa araw!
basic.es: ¡Piel negra bajo pelaje blanco absorbe calor solar!

edu.en: They can smell seals through 3 ft of snow and swim for days between ice.
edu.tl: Amoy ng seal sa 3 ft na niyebe at lumalangoy ng araw-araw sa pagitan ng yelo.
edu.es: Huelen focas bajo 3 pies de nieve y nadan días entre hielo.

basic.en: Arctic foxes turn snow-white in winter and brown in summer—seasonal outfit swap!
basic.tl: Puti sa taglamig at kayumanggi sa tag-init ang arctic fox—seasonal outfit!
basic.es: ¡Blancos en invierno y marrones en verano, cambio de outfit!

edu.en: They follow polar bears to eat leftover seal meals on the ice.
edu.tl: Sumusunod sa polar bear para kumain ng tirang seal.
edu.es: Siguen osos polares por restos de focas.

basic.en: Arctic hares sprint up to 40 mph in snow—white lightning with long legs!
basic.tl: Tumatakbo ng 40 mph ang arctic hare sa niyebe—white lightning!
basic.es: ¡Corren 40 mph en nieve, rayos blancos!

edu.en: Thick fur and short ears reduce heat loss in freezing winds.
edu.tl: Makapal na balahibo at maikling tainga para hindi mawala ang init.
edu.es: Pelaje grueso y orejas cortas evitan pérdida de calor.

diet.tl: Halaman, balat ng puno, at sanga (herbivoro)

basic.en: An Arctic wolf can vanish in blowing snow with its white coat, moving so quietly that the tundra feels like it’s holding its breath.
basic.tl: Kayang maglaho ng Arctic wolf sa umiihip na niyebe dahil sa puting balahibo, at sobrang tahimik kumilos na parang humihinto ang hininga ng tundra.
basic.es: El lobo ártico puede desaparecer en la nieve con su pelaje blanco y moverse tan silencioso que la tundra parece contener la respiración.

edu.en: Smaller packs roam vast tundra following caribou migrations.
edu.tl: Mas maliit na pack sa tundra kasunod ng caribou.
edu.es: Manadas siguen migraciones de caribú en tundra.

region.es: Ártico alto de Canadá y Groenlandia

basic.en: Reindeer hooves spread like snowshoes, helping them walk on soft snow, and their noses warm icy air before each breath.
basic.tl: Kumakalat na parang snowshoes ang kuko ng reindeer para makalakad sa malambot na niyebe, at pinapainit ng ilong ang sobrang lamig na hangin.
basic.es: Las pezuñas del reno se abren como raquetas para caminar sobre nieve blanda, y su nariz calienta el aire helado antes de respirar.

edu.en: Hooves widen in winter like snowshoes and shrink in spring.
edu.tl: Lumalapad ang kuko sa taglamig parang snowshoe at lumiliit sa tagsibol.
edu.es: Pezuñas se ensanchan en invierno como raquetas.

basic.en: Caribou herds migrate farther than any land mammal—thousands of miles a year!
basic.tl: Pinakamahaba ang migration ng caribou—libo-libong milya taun-taon!
basic.es: ¡Migran más que cualquier mamífero terrestre, miles de millas!

edu.en: Both males and females grow antlers; hooves click on tundra walks.
edu.tl: Parehong may sungay; kumakaluskos ang kuko sa tundra.
edu.es: Machos y hembras tienen astas; pezuñas hacen clic en tundra.

region.tl: Arctic at subarctic na Hilagang Amerika

basic.en: Musk oxen circle babies in a shield wall when wolves attack—living fortress!
basic.tl: Bilog ang musk ox para protektahan ang sanggol kapag umaatake ang wolf—living fortress!
basic.es: ¡Forman muralla viva protegiendo crías de lobos!

edu.en: Their qiviut underwool is eight times warmer than sheep wool.
edu.tl: 8 beses na mas mainit ang qiviut kaysa lana ng tupa.
edu.es: La lana qiviut es ocho veces más cálida que la de oveja.

basic.en: Lemmings don't really jump off cliffs—they boom and bust in population cycles!
basic.tl: Hindi talaga tumatalon sa bangin ang lemming—boom at bust ang populasyon!
basic.es: ¡No se tiran de acantilados; ciclos de población boom-bust!

edu.en: They tunnel under snow all winter eating roots without surfacing.
edu.tl: Nasa ilalim ng niyebe buong taglamig kumakain ng ugat.
edu.es: Túneles bajo nieve comiendo raíces todo el invierno.

basic.en: Walruses use giant tusks to pull onto ice and to hug friends in crowded haul-outs!
basic.tl: Ginagamit ang tusks para umakyat sa yelo at yumakap sa kaibigan!
basic.es: ¡Colmillos gigantes suben al hielo y saludan amigos!

edu.en: Whiskers feel for clams on the seafloor; they suck shells clean in one gulp.
edu.tl: Ramdam ng whiskers ang clam sa ilalim; nilalamon ang shell sa isang lagok.
edu.es: Bigotes detectan almejas; las succionan enteras.

habitat.en: Arctic ice floes and shallow seas
habitat.tl: Arctic ice floe at mababaw na dagat
habitat.es: Hielo flotante ártico y mares someros

basic.en: Harp seal pups have fluffy white coats for two weeks—snowball babies on ice!
basic.tl: Puting fluffy coat ang batang harp seal ng dalawang linggo—snowball baby!
basic.es: ¡Crías blancas esponjosas dos semanas en el hielo!

edu.en: Adults migrate following pack ice and dive 1,000 ft for fish.
edu.tl: Naglalakbay kasunod ng pack ice at sumisid ng 1,000 ft para sa isda.
edu.es: Migran siguiendo hielo y bucean 1,000 pies por peces.

habitat.tl: Pack ice at malamig na karagatan

basic.en: Harbor seals banana-pose on rocks to keep flippers dry and warm!
basic.tl: Naka-banana pose ang harbor seal sa bato para tuyo at mainit ang flipper!
basic.es: ¡Posa plátano en rocas para secar aletas!

edu.en: They can sleep underwater for minutes, surfacing automatically to breathe.
edu.tl: Natutulog sa ilalim ng tubig at awtomatikong lumalabas para huminga.
edu.es: Duermen bajo agua y suben automáticamente a respirar.

basic.en: Belugas are white 'sea canaries' that chirp, whistle, and mimic sounds!
basic.tl: Puting 'sea canary' ang beluga na humihiyaw at gumagaya ng tunog!
basic.es: ¡Ballenas blancas 'canarios marinos' que imitan sonidos!

edu.en: Flexible necks turn heads unlike most whales; they molt skin in summer.
edu.tl: Lumilikot ang ulo; nagbabago ng balat sa tag-init.
edu.es: Cuello flexible gira la cabeza; mudan piel en verano.

region.tl: Arctic Ocean at kalapit na dagat

basic.en: Narwhals grow unicorn tusks up to 10 ft—the tusk is actually a spiral tooth!
basic.tl: May unicorn tusk hanggang 10 ft ang narwhal—spiral tooth pala!
basic.es: ¡Colmillo unicornio de 10 pies, un diente en espiral!

edu.en: Tusks sense salt and temperature; males may use them in friendly jousting.
edu.tl: Nararamdaman ng tusk ang asin at init; ginagamit din sa friendly joust.
edu.es: El colmillo detecta sal y temperatura; los machos duelan amistosamente.

habitat.tl: Gilid ng yelo sa Arctic Ocean

basic.en: Bowheads may live over 200 years—the oldest mammals on Earth!
basic.tl: Maaaring 200+ taon ang bowhead—pinakamatandang mammal sa Earth!
basic.es: ¡Pueden vivir más de 200 años, los mamíferos más longevos!

edu.en: Massive skull breaks through ice to breathe; baleen filters tiny prey.
edu.tl: Malaking bungo para basagin ang yelo; baleen ang nagfi-filter ng pagkain.
edu.es: Cráneos rompen hielo; barbas filtran presas diminutas.

diet.tl: Krill at maliliit na crustacean (filter feeder)

basic.en: Sperm whales dive 3,000 ft hunting giant squid—deep-sea monster battles!
basic.tl: Sumisid ng 3,000 ft ang sperm whale para sa giant squid—monster battle!
basic.es: ¡Bucean 3,000 pies cazando calamares gigantes!

edu.en: The huge head holds spermaceti oil once used in lamps; they click to echolocate.
edu.tl: Malaking ulo na may spermaceti; echolocation click para hanapin ang pusit.
edu.es: Cabeza enorme con aceite spermaceti; clics de ecolocalización.

habitat.tl: Malalim na karagatan sa buong mundo

basic.en: Male elephant seals roar and jiggle noses in epic beach battles!
basic.tl: Umuungol at ginagalaw ang ilong ang lalaking elephant seal sa beach battle!
basic.es: ¡Machos rugen y mueven la trompa en batallas épicas!

edu.en: They dive 5,000 ft for squid and can hold breath over an hour.
edu.tl: Sumisid ng 5,000 ft para sa pusit at kaya ang isang oras na walang hininga.
edu.es: Bucean 5,000 pies por calamares; aguantan respiración una hora.

region.tl: Southern oceans at baybayin ng California
region.es: Océano Austral y costa de California

basic.en: Leopard seals have spotted coats and grinning faces, ruling icy waters as top hunters with a splash of drama.
basic.tl: May batik na balahibo ang leopard seal at nakangiting mukha—top hunter sa yelo na may kaunting drama.
basic.es: Las focas leopardo tienen manchas y sonrisa amplia, reinando en aguas heladas como cazadoras con drama.

edu.en: They eat penguins by shaking them fiercely at the water's edge.
edu.tl: Kinakalabit ang penguin sa gilid ng tubig.
edu.es: Sacuden pingüinos en la orilla del agua.

basic.en: Weddell seals sing underwater songs that bounce through Antarctic ice, like chilly karaoke stars.
basic.tl: Kumakanta ang Weddell seal sa ilalim ng tubig at umaalingawngaw sa yelo—parang karaoke star sa Antartiko.
basic.es: Las focas Weddell cantan bajo el hielo antártico, como estrellas de karaoke heladas.

edu.en: They keep breathing holes open in ice with teeth and dive 2,000 ft.
edu.tl: Pinapanatiling bukas ang butas sa yelo gamit ang ngipin; sumisid ng 2,000 ft.
edu.es: Mantienen agujeros en hielo con dientes; bucean 2,000 pies.

basic.en: Emperor dads balance eggs on feet for two months in Antarctic blizzards!
basic.tl: Dadalangin ng emperor dad ang itlog sa paa ng dalawang buwan sa blizzard!
basic.es: ¡Papás equilibran huevos en pies dos meses en ventiscas!

edu.en: They dive over 1,800 ft for fish—the deepest of any bird.
edu.tl: Sumisid ng 1,800+ ft para sa isda—pinakamalalim sa lahat ng ibon.
edu.es: Bucean 1,800 pies, récord entre aves.

basic.en: Adélie penguins steal pebbles from neighbors' nests—naughty rock collectors!
basic.tl: Nagnanakaw ng pebble ang Adélie mula sa nest ng kapitbahay!
basic.es: ¡Roban piedras de nidos vecinos, coleccionistas traviesos!

edu.en: They toboggan on bellies over ice faster than walking.
edu.tl: Dumudulas sa tiyan sa yelo na mas mabilis kaysa lakad.
edu.es: Se deslizan panza abajo más rápido que caminan.

basic.en: Rockhoppers hop up cliffs with spiky yellow crests like punk rock feathers!
basic.tl: Tumatalon sa bangin ang rockhopper na may spiky yellow crest—punk rock feathers!
basic.es: ¡Saltan acantilados con crestas amarillas punk!

edu.en: They nest on steep rocky islands in noisy crowded colonies.
edu.tl: Nangitlog sa matarik na batong isla sa maingay na kolonya.
edu.es: Anidan en islas rocosas empinadas en colonias ruidosas.

basic.en: Antarctic krill swarm in pink clouds so huge that satellites can spot them from space—ocean glitter with legs.
basic.tl: Nagsasama-sama ang Antarctic krill na parang pink cloud na nakikita pa ng satellite—parang kumikinang na glitter ng dagat.
basic.es: El krill antártico forma nubes rosadas tan grandes que los satélites las ven desde el espacio: purpurina del océano.

edu.en: They feed whales, penguins, and seals—the keystone of Antarctic food webs.
edu.tl: Pagkain ng whale, penguin, at seal—keystone ng Antarctic food web.
edu.es: Alimentan ballenas, pingüinos y focas; piedra angular antártica.

basic.en: Snow geese fly in huge white Vs that look like rivers in the sky!
basic.tl: Lumilipad ang snow goose sa malaking puting V parang ilog sa langit!
basic.es: ¡V blancas gigantes como ríos en el cielo!

edu.en: They nest in Arctic tundra then migrate thousands of miles to warmer fields.
edu.tl: Nangitlog sa Arctic tundra tapos libo-libong milya papuntang mainit na bukirin.
edu.es: Anidan en tundra ártica y migran miles de millas al sur.

basic.en: Ptarmigan swap to snow-white feathers in winter and grow extra feathery snowshoes on their feet for fluffy tundra walks.
basic.tl: Nagiging puti ang ptarmigan sa taglamig at lumalaki ang feather sa paa na parang snowshoes sa tundra.
basic.es: El lagópodo se vuelve blanco en invierno y crece plumas en los pies como raquetas para caminar en la tundra.

edu.en: Feathered feet act like snow boots in Arctic and mountain habitats.
edu.tl: Balahibo sa paa parang snow boot sa Arctic at bundok.
edu.es: Patas emplumadas como botas de nieve.

basic.en: Puffins carry dozens of fish crosswise in colorful beaks—clown birds of the sea!
basic.tl: Dosenang isda ang dala ng puffin sa makulay na tuka—clown bird ng dagat!
basic.es: ¡Docenas de peces en picos coloridos, payasos del mar!

edu.en: They dig burrow nests on cliffs and flap wings 400 times a minute to fly.
edu.tl: Nangitlog sa lungga sa bangin; 400 flap bawat minuto para lumipad.
edu.es: Madrigueras en acantilados; 400 aleteos por minuto al volar.

habitat.tl: Bangin at karagatan ng Hilagang Atlantic
habitat.es: Acantilados y océano del Atlántico norte

region.tl: Baybayin ng Hilagang Atlantic at Arctic
region.es: Costas del Atlántico norte y Ártico

basic.en: Clownfish live safely in stinging anemones—best friends with a squishy fortress!
basic.tl: Nakatira ang clownfish sa stinging anemone—best friends sa squishy fortress!
basic.es: ¡Viven en anémonas urticantes, amigos de fortaleza blanda!

edu.en: All are born male; dominant fish can become female to lead the group.
edu.tl: Lalaki lahat ng ipinanganak; maaaring maging babae ang lider.
edu.es: Nacen machos; el dominante puede volverse hembra.

basic.en: Angelfish glide through coral like flat golden frisbees, turning corners with fins that sparkle in sunbeams.
basic.tl: Dumadaan ang angelfish sa coral na parang flat golden frisbee, kumukutkot sa sulok na kumikislap sa araw.
basic.es: Los peces ángel cruzan el coral como frisbees dorados planos que brillan al girar en los rayos de sol.

edu.en: Juveniles act as cleaners picking parasites off bigger fish.
edu.tl: Naglilinis ang batang angelfish ng parasite sa malaking isda.
edu.es: Crías limpian parásitos de peces grandes.

diet.tl: Sponge, algae, at maliliit na invertebrate (omniboro)

weight.en: Mostly water; few oz to lbs

basic.en: Jellyfish drift without bones or brains, pulsing like living lava lamps that sting if you boop the wrong tentacle.
basic.tl: Lumulutang ang jellyfish na walang buto at utak, parang living lava lamp na masakit kapag maling tentacle ang natamaan.
basic.es: Las medusas flotan sin huesos ni cerebro, pulsando como lámparas vivas que pican si tocas el tentáculo equivocado.

edu.en: Some sting with venom cells; others glow with bioluminescence.
edu.tl: May venom cell; may bioluminescence.
edu.es: Algunas pican con células venenosas; otras brillan.

basic.en: Starfish can regrow lost arms—some even grow a whole new body from one arm!
basic.tl: Kayang ipagdugtong ng starfish ang nawawalang braso—minsan buong katawan!
basic.es: ¡Regeneran brazos; a veces un brazo crea cuerpo nuevo!

edu.en: They push stomachs out of mouths to digest clams outside their shells.
edu.tl: Inilalabas ang tiyan para tunawin ang clam sa labas ng shell.
edu.es: Sacan el estómago para digerir almejas fuera del caparazón.

weight.tl: Mas mababa sa 1 oz hanggang 1 lb

basic.en: Male seahorses carry babies in a pouch—dads do the pregnancy!
basic.tl: Nasa pouch ng lalaking seahorse ang sanggol—tatay ang buntis!
basic.es: ¡Los papás llevan crías en bolsa, embarazo paterno!

edu.en: They anchor with curly tails and suck prey through snout like a straw.
edu.tl: Kumakapit sa curly tail at sinisipsip ang biktima sa snout.
edu.es: Se sujetan con cola rizada y succionan presas.

basic.en: Sea turtles navigate for decades and return to the same beach where they hatched—GPS hearts with flippers.
basic.tl: Naglalakbay ang sea turtle nang dekada at bumabalik sa parehong beach kung saan ipinanganak—parang GPS na may flippers.
basic.es: Las tortugas marinas viajan décadas y vuelven a la playa donde nacieron: corazones GPS con aletas.

edu.en: They cry salty tears to flush extra salt from drinking seawater.
edu.tl: Umiiyak ng maalat na luha para tanggalin ang asin mula sa dagat.
edu.es: Lágrimas saladas eliminan exceso de sal del agua de mar.

region.tl: Tropikal at temperate na karagatan sa buong mundo

basic.en: Lobsters taste with their legs and can live longer than a century—clawed grandmas of the seafloor.
basic.tl: Namamalasahan ng lobster gamit ang binti at maaaring umabot ng isang siglo—parang lola na may pangil sa ilalim ng dagat.
basic.es: Las langostas prueban con las patas y pueden vivir más de un siglo: abuelas con pinzas del fondo marino.

edu.en: They molt shells to grow, hiding until new armor hardens.
edu.tl: Nagbabago ng shell para lumaki; nagtatago hanggang matigas ang bagong armor.
edu.es: Mudan caparazón para crecer; se esconden hasta endurecer.

basic.en: Hermit crabs swap shells as they grow—mobile homes on the beach!
basic.tl: Nagpapalit ng shell ang hermit crab habang lumalaki—mobile home!
basic.es: ¡Cambian conchas al crecer, casas móviles!

edu.en: Soft abdomen stays protected inside borrowed snail shells.
edu.tl: Protektado ang malambot na tiyan sa hiniram na shell.
edu.es: Abdomen blando protegido en conchas prestadas.

region.tl: Tropikal at temperate na baybayin sa buong mundo

heightLength.tl: Kolonya ng ilang ft ang lapad
heightLength.es: Colonias de varios pies de ancho

basic.en: Brain corals grow wrinkly mounds that look like giant thinking caps, building reef cities one bump at a time.
basic.tl: Lumalaki ang brain coral na parang giant thinking cap, nagtatayo ng reef city isang bump kada beses.
basic.es: Los corales cerebro forman montículos arrugados como gorras gigantes, construyendo ciudades de arrecife.

edu.en: Polyps catch plankton at night while algae inside give them energy by day.
edu.tl: Hinuhuli ang plankton gabi; algae sa loob nagbibigay enerhiya araw.
edu.es: Pólipos cazan plancton de noche; algas simbióticas dan energía de día.

region.tl: Tropikal na karagatan sa buong mundo

basic.en: Sea anemones look like flowers but sting like traps—pretty and powerful!
basic.tl: Mukhang bulaklak ang sea anemone pero sumisipol—pretty at powerful!
basic.es: ¡Parecen flores pero pican como trampas!

edu.en: Stinging cells paralyze small fish that wander into tentacles.
edu.tl: Pinaparalisa ng sting cell ang maliliit na isda.
edu.es: Células urticantes paralizan peces pequeños.

basic.en: Butterflyfish swim in bright pairs through coral, flashing stripes like underwater butterflies on a date.
basic.tl: Lumalangoy ang butterflyfish na magkapares sa coral, kumikislap ang guhit na parang butterflies na may date sa ilalim ng dagat.
basic.es: Los peces mariposa nadan en parejas brillantes por el coral, como mariposas submarinas en una cita.

edu.en: Many eat coral polyps and defend reef territories with mates.
edu.tl: Kumakain ng coral polyp at pinoprotektahan ang territoryo kasama ang asawa.
edu.es: Comen pólipos y defienden territorio con pareja.

diet.tl: Coral polyp at maliliit na invertebrate (karnivoro)

basic.en: Blue tangs zip through reefs in electric blue with sunny tail tips, like swimming highlighters with fins.
basic.tl: Tumatakbo ang blue tang sa reef na parang electric blue na highlighter na may pakpak at dilaw na dulo ng buntot.
basic.es: Los peces cirujano azules cruzan el arrecife en azul eléctrico con cola soleada, como marcadores que nadan.

edu.en: Sharp spine on tail can pop out for defense when threatened.
edu.tl: Matutulis na spine sa buntot para depensa kapag nanganganib.
edu.es: Espina en cola se despliega al amenazarse.

basic.en: Mandarinfish shimmer with psychedelic colors—tiny dragons of the reef!
basic.tl: Kumikislap ang mandarinfish na parang psychedelic—tiny dragon ng reef!
basic.es: ¡Colores psicodélicos, mini dragones del arrecife!

edu.en: They secrete mucus that smells bad to predators instead of scales.
edu.tl: May mucus na mabaho para sa predator imbes na kaliskis.
edu.es: Moco maloliente en lugar de escamas repele depredadores.

basic.en: Moorish idols trail a long ribbon fin behind them, swimming like dressed-up reef royalty in a fancy underwater parade.
basic.tl: May mahabang ribbon fin na sumusunod sa moorish idol, lumalangoy na parang bihis-na-bihis na royalty ng reef sa isang fancy underwater parade.
basic.es: Los ídolos moro llevan una aleta larga como cinta y nadan como realeza elegante del arrecife en un desfile submarino.

edu.en: Bold black and yellow stripes make them icons of tropical reefs.
edu.tl: Itim at dilaw na guhit—icon ng tropikal na reef.
edu.es: Rayas negras y amarillas, iconos de arrecifes.

basic.en: Parrotfish crunch coral with beak teeth and poop sand—beach builders!
basic.tl: Durugin ng parrotfish ang coral at nagiging buhangin—beach builder!
basic.es: ¡Trituran coral y excretan arena, hacedores de playas!

edu.en: Some wrap in mucus cocoons at night to hide scent from predators.
edu.tl: Nagbabalot ng mucus cocoon gabi para itago ang amoy.
edu.es: Capullos de moco nocturnos ocultan olor.

region.tl: Tropikal na karagatan sa buong mundo

basic.en: Triggerfish lock their dorsal spine like a safety switch, then bulldoze through reefs with feisty attitudes.
basic.tl: Naka-lock ang dorsal spine ng triggerfish na parang safety switch, tapos rumaragasa sa reef na may feisty attitude.
basic.es: Los peces ballesta bloquean su espina dorsal como interruptor y avanzan por el arrecife con actitud valiente.

edu.en: They dig nests in sand and fiercely guard eggs from intruders.
edu.tl: Humuhukay ng nest sa buhangin at agresibong nagbabantay ng itlog.
edu.es: Excavan nidos en arena y defienden huevos ferozmente.

basic.en: Lionfish fan venomous spines like underwater lions with fancy manes!
basic.tl: Venomous spine parang underwater lion na may fancy mane!
basic.es: ¡Espinas venenosas como melenas de león submarino!

edu.en: Invasive in Atlantic but native to Pacific where predators keep numbers down.
edu.tl: Invasive sa Atlantic; sa Pacific may predator na kumokontrol.
edu.es: Invasor en Atlántico; en Pacífico depredadores lo controlan.

basic.en: Manta rays glide like flying carpets and leap entirely out of the water!
basic.tl: Lumulutang parang flying carpet at tumatalon sa tubig!
basic.es: ¡Planan como alfombras voladoras y saltan del agua!

edu.en: They have the largest brain of any fish and visit cleaning stations.
edu.tl: Pinakamalaking utak sa lahat ng isda; pumupunta sa cleaning station.
edu.es: Cerebro más grande entre peces; visitan estaciones de limpieza.

basic.en: Stingrays hide under sand with only eyes peeking out, like shy pancakes waiting to whoosh away.
basic.tl: Nagtatago ang stingray sa buhangin na mata lang ang kita—parang mahiyain na pancake na biglang aalis.
basic.es: Las rayas se entierran en arena con solo los ojos afuera, como panqueques tímidos listos para deslizarse.

edu.en: Tail spine delivers venom if stepped on; shuffling feet warns them away.
edu.tl: May venom sa buntot kapag natapakan; kumuskos ng paa para umalis.
edu.es: Espina venenosa si se pisan; arrastrar pies las espanta.

region.tl: Mainit na karagatan sa buong mundo

basic.en: Reef sharks zip through coral alleys like quiet lifeguards, keeping the reef healthy by chasing sick fish away.
basic.tl: Dumaraan ang reef shark sa mga “eskinita” ng coral na parang tahimik na lifeguard, tinutulungan ang reef sa paghabol ng mahihinang isda.
basic.es: Los tiburones de arrecife pasan por callejones de coral como salvavidas silenciosos, ayudando al arrecife al perseguir peces enfermos.

edu.en: Usually shy toward humans; important apex predators on reefs.
edu.tl: Mahiyain sa tao; mahalagang apex predator sa reef.
edu.es: Tímidos con humanos; depredadores ápice del arrecife.

basic.en: Hammerhead sharks have T-shaped heads that work like stereo sonar!
basic.tl: T-shaped na ulo parang stereo sonar ang hammerhead shark!
basic.es: ¡Cabezas en T como sonar estéreo!

edu.en: Wide-set eyes give 360° vision; they hunt stingrays in sandy flats.
edu.tl: 360° vision at nanghuhuli ng stingray sa buhangin.
edu.es: Visión 360° cazando rayas en planicies.

region.tl: Mainit na karagatan sa buong mundo

basic.en: Great whites breach like missiles launching at seals!
basic.tl: Tumatalon ang great white parang missile papunta sa seal!
basic.es: ¡Saltan como misiles hacia focas!

edu.en: Electroreceptors detect heartbeat of hidden prey in murky water.
edu.tl: Nararamdaman ang tibok ng puso ng nakatagong biktima.
edu.es: Detectan latidos de presas ocultas.

region.tl: Malamig na karagatan sa buong mundo

basic.en: Tiger sharks wear stripe patterns when young and will try almost any snack—including things that definitely aren’t fish food.
basic.tl: May guhit ang tiger shark noong bata at susubukan halos lahat ng pagkain—kahit hindi talaga pagkain ng isda.
basic.es: Los tiburones tigre tienen rayas de jóvenes y prueban casi cualquier bocado, incluso cosas que no son comida.

edu.en: Serrated teeth cut through turtle shells and bone.
edu.tl: Tinutudla ng ngipin ang shell ng pagong at buto.
edu.es: Dientes dentados cortan caparazones y huesos.

region.tl: Mainit na karagatan sa buong mundo

basic.en: Sea urchins are spiky pincushions that graze algae with a five-tooth mouth hidden under all those needles.
basic.tl: Spiky pincushion ang sea urchin na kumakain ng algae gamit ang five-tooth mouth sa ilalim ng tinik.
basic.es: Los erizos de mar son almohadillas con púas que comen algas con una boca de cinco dientes escondida.

edu.en: Spines move on ball joints; some species have venomous tips.
edu.tl: Gumagalaw ang tinik; may venomous tip ang ilan.
edu.es: Púas móviles; algunas puntas venenosas.

habitat.tl: Batong reef at ilalim ng dagat

basic.en: Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood—and solve puzzles to open jars!
basic.tl: May tatlong puso at asul na dugo ang octopus—nabubuksan ang garapon!
basic.es: ¡Tres corazones, sangre azul y abren frascos con puzzles!

edu.en: They change skin color and texture in milliseconds using millions of pigment cells.
edu.tl: Nagbabago ng kulay at texture sa milliseconds gamit ang milyon-milyong pigment cell.
edu.es: Cambian color y textura en milisegundos con millones de células.

basic.en: Cuttlefish ripple colors across their skin like living TV screens!
basic.tl: Kumikislap ang kulay sa balat parang living TV screen!
basic.es: ¡Ondas de color en la piel como pantallas vivas!

edu.en: Internal cuttlebone helps control buoyancy; W-shaped pupils see polarized light.
edu.tl: Cuttlebone para buoyancy; W-shaped pupil nakakakita ng polarized light.
edu.es: Hueso de sepia regula flotabilidad; pupilas en W ven luz polarizada.

region.tl: Temperate at tropikal na dagat sa buong mundo

basic.en: Moray eels open and close their mouths to breathe—it looks scary, but it’s just underwater gulping, not a mean face.
basic.tl: Binubuksan at isinasara ng moray eel ang bibig para huminga—mukhang nakakatakot pero gulping lang sa tubig.
basic.es: Las morenas abren y cierran la boca para respirar: parece furia, pero solo tragan agua, no es mala cara.

edu.en: Pharyngeal jaws in the throat shoot forward to grab prey inside caves.
edu.tl: May pharyngeal jaw sa lalamunan na lumalabas para habulin ang biktima sa kuweba.
edu.es: Mandíbulas faríngeas atrapan presas dentro de cuevas.

basic.en: Pufferfish puff up like spiky balloons when scared—don't-pop ocean balls!
basic.tl: Lumalaki ang pufferfish parang spiky balloon kapag takot!
basic.es: ¡Se inflan como globos espinosos al asustarse!

edu.en: Some species make nest circles in sand and males guard eggs fiercely.
edu.tl: Gumagawa ng bilog sa buhangin at binabantayan ng lalaki ang itlog.
edu.es: Machos hacen círculos en arena y guardan huevos.

basic.en: Dolphins call each other by signature whistles—each has a unique name!
basic.tl: May signature whistle ang dolphin—may sariling pangalan!
basic.es: ¡Cada delfín tiene un silbido-nombre único!

edu.en: They sleep with half their brain awake so they can breathe at the surface.
edu.tl: Kalahating utak ang gising habang natutulog para makahinga sa ibabaw.
edu.es: Duermen con medio cerebro despierto para respirar.

region.tl: Temperate at tropikal na karagatan sa buong mundo

basic.en: Orcas wear black-and-white tuxedo coats and hunt in tight family pods, chatting with clicks that sound like underwater Morse code.
basic.tl: Itim-puti ang orca na parang tuxedo, mangangaso kasama ang pamilya, nag-uusap sa clicks na parang Morse code sa tubig.
basic.es: Las orcas lucen esmoquin blanco y negro, cazan en familia y hablan con clics como código Morse submarino.

edu.en: Different pods use unique hunting cultures, like wave-washing seals off ice.
edu.tl: May unique hunting culture ang bawat pod, tulad ng wave-washing sa seal.
edu.es: Manadas tienen culturas de caza como empujar focas del hielo.

habitat.en: All oceans from Arctic to Antarctic
habitat.tl: Lahat ng karagatan mula Arctic hanggang Antarctic
habitat.es: Todos los océanos de Ártico a Antártida

basic.en: Sea lions bark and clap their flippers on rocks like aquatic performers!
basic.tl: Umuungol at pumapalakpak ang sea lion sa bato parang aquatic performer!
basic.es: ¡Ladran y aplauden con aletas como artistas marinos!

edu.en: Front flippers rotate forward so they can walk on land faster than seals.
edu.tl: Umiikot ang front flipper para makalakad sa lupa nang mas mabilis kaysa seal.
edu.es: Aletas delanteras les permiten caminar en tierra más que focas.

region.tl: Baybayin ng Pacific sa buong mundo

basic.en: Sea otters float on backs cracking shellfish with rocks—cute tool users!
basic.tl: Lumulutang ang sea otter habang binabasag ang shellfish sa bato!
basic.es: ¡Flotan boca arriba rompiendo mariscos con piedras!

edu.en: Densest fur of any animal traps air for warmth; they eat 25% body weight daily.
edu.tl: Pinakampis na balahibo; kumakain ng 25% ng timbang araw-araw.
edu.es: Pelaje más denso atrapa aire; comen 25% de su peso diario.

basic.en: Goblin sharks shoot jaws forward like alien monsters—living fossils!
basic.tl: Pinuputok ang panga ng goblin shark parang alien monster!
basic.es: ¡Mandíbulas se proyectan como monstruo alienígena!

edu.en: Pink skin and blade-like snout detect prey with electroreceptors.
edu.tl: Pink na balat at blade snout na electroreceptor.
edu.es: Piel rosada y hocico detector de presas.

region.en: Deep Atlantic, Pacific, and Indian oceans
