region.tl: Malalim na Atlantic, Pacific, at Indian

basic.en: Frilled sharks look like sea serpents with frilly gill slits!
basic.tl: Mukhang sea serpent na may frilly gill slit ang frilled shark!
basic.es: ¡Serpientes marinas con branquias fruncidas!

edu.en: 300 trident-shaped teeth trap squid in sudden lunges.
edu.tl: 300 trident na ngipin para huliin ang pusit.
edu.es: 300 dientes tridente atrapan calamares.

habitat.tl: Malalim na karagatan malapit sa ilalim

basic.en: Dragonfish flash red and blue lights invisible to most prey—secret night vision battle!
basic.tl: Pulang asul na ilaw na hindi nakikita ng biktima—secret night vision!
basic.es: ¡Luces rojas y azules invisibles para presas!

edu.en: Huge teeth don't fit in mouth, sticking out like fangs.
edu.tl: Hindi kasya ang ngipin sa bibig—nakalabas parang fang.
edu.es: Dientes enormes sobresalen como colmillos.

habitat.tl: Twilight zone ng malalim na karagatan

region.tl: Malalim na tropikal at temperate na dagat

basic.en: Viperfish flash needle fangs so long they barely fit, lighting up the dark to scare snacks into surprise.
basic.tl: May needle fangs ang viperfish na halos hindi kasya—nagpapailaw sa dilim para magulat ang biktima.
basic.es: El pez víbora muestra colmillos larguísimos y brilla en la oscuridad para asustar a su comida.

edu.en: Photophores along the body flash signals and lure prey in the abyss.
edu.tl: Kumikislap ang photophore sa katawan para akitin ang biktima.
edu.es: Fotóforos en el cuerpo atraen presas en el abismo.

basic.en: Fangtooth fish pack the biggest teeth for their size in the ocean—a grin that could win a scary contest.
basic.tl: Pinakamalaking ngipin sa itsura ang fangtooth—ngiting panalo sa scary contest sa dagat.
basic.es: El pez colmillo tiene los dientes más grandes para su tamaño: una sonrisa campeona de miedo.

edu.en: Despite fierce look, it eats tiny crustaceans in deep darkness.
edu.tl: Sa kabila ng fierce look, maliliit na crustacean ang kinakain.
edu.es: A pesar del aspecto, come crustáceos diminutos.

region.tl: Tropikal at temperate na malalim na tubig

basic.en: Gulper eels swallow prey bigger than themselves—living parachute mouths!
basic.tl: Nilalamon ng gulper eel ang mas malaki sa sarili—parachute mouth!
basic.es: ¡Tragan presas más grandes que ellos, bocas paracaídas!

edu.en: Tail tip glows pink to lure fish into expandable jaws.
edu.tl: Kumikinang pink ang dulo ng buntot para akitin ang isda.
edu.es: Punta de cola rosa atrae peces a mandíbulas expandibles.

region.tl: Tropikal at temperate na malalim na dagat

basic.en: Hatchetfish are shaped like silver hatchets with glowing bellies, flashing lights to chat in the midnight sea.
basic.tl: Hugis silver hatchet ang hatchetfish na may kumikislap na tiyan—nagpapailaw para mag-usap sa dagat.
basic.es: Los peces hacha parecen hachas plateadas con vientres brillantes que parpadean para hablar en el mar nocturno.

edu.en: Bioluminescent bellies hide their shadow from predators below.
edu.tl: Itinatago ng kumikinang tiyan ang anino mula sa predator sa ibaba.
edu.es: Vientres bioluminiscentes ocultan su sombra.

basic.en: Lanternfish sparkle with belly lights in the deep sea, and there are so many they might be the most common fish on Earth.
basic.tl: Kumikislap ang lanternfish sa tiyan sa malalim na dagat—posibleng pinakamaraming uri ng isda sa Earth.
basic.es: Los peces linterna brillan en el vientre en el mar profundo y quizá sean los peces más comunes del planeta.

edu.en: They migrate upward nightly in huge swarms feeding the ocean food web.
edu.tl: Umaakyat gabi-gabi sa malaking kawan para pakainin ang food web.
edu.es: Migran arriba cada noche en cardúmenes enormes.

habitat.tl: Twilight zone ng malalim na karagatan

basic.en: Barreleye fish have see-through heads so you can peek at their green tube eyes—like a fish with a glass helmet.
basic.tl: See-through ang ulo ng barreleye kaya kitang-kita ang green tube eyes—parang isda na may glass helmet.
basic.es: El pez cabeza transparente tiene cráneo claro y ojos verdes en tubo, como casco de vidrio.

edu.en: Eyes point upward to spot prey silhouettes while head shields them.
edu.tl: Tumingin pataas ang mata habang pinoprotektahan ng ulo.
edu.es: Ojos miran arriba detectando siluetas; cabeza los protege.

region.tl: Malalim na tubig ng Hilagang Pacific

basic.en: Coffinfish inflate like spiky balloons and walk on fin-feet on the seafloor!
basic.tl: Lumulaki ang coffinfish parang spiky balloon at naglalakad sa fin-feet!
basic.es: ¡Se inflan como globos espinosos y caminan con aletas!

edu.en: They lure prey with a rod on the head like anglerfish cousins.
edu.tl: Inaakit ang biktima gamit ang rod sa ulo parang anglerfish.
edu.es: Señuelo en la cabeza como los rapez.

basic.en: Oarfish are ribbon-thin sea serpents that wash ashore and start sea monster legends!
basic.tl: Ribbon-thin sea serpent ang oarfish—simula ng sea monster legend!
basic.es: ¡Serpientes marinas delgadas originan leyendas de monstruos!

edu.en: They swim vertically eating tiny plankton despite giant size.
edu.tl: Lumalangoy patayo at kumakain ng plankton kahit higante.
edu.es: Nadan verticalmente comiendo plancton pese a su tamaño.

basic.en: Colossal squids have swiveling hooks on tentacles—the kraken is real!
basic.tl: May umiikot na hook sa tentacles ang colossal squid—totoo ang kraken!
basic.es: ¡Ganchos giratorios en tentáculos, el kraken es real!

edu.en: Eyes the size of dinner plates are the largest in the animal kingdom.
edu.tl: Kasinglaki ng plato ang mata—pinakamalaki sa animal kingdom.
edu.es: Ojos del tamaño de platos, los más grandes del reino animal.

region.es: Océano Austral alrededor de la Antártida

basic.en: Cookiecutter sharks bite round plugs out of whales, submarines, and even sonar!
basic.tl: Kumakagat ng bilog na plug ang cookiecutter shark sa whale, submarine, at sonar!
basic.es: ¡Muerden trozos circulares de ballenas y submarinos!

edu.en: Glowing belly patches trick prey into approaching from below.
edu.tl: Kumikinang ang tiyan para lokohin ang biktima mula sa ibaba.
edu.es: Vientre luminoso atrae presas desde abajo.

basic.en: Six-gill sharks are ancient deep swimmers with an extra gill slit, like sharks wearing a bonus breathing pocket.
basic.tl: Sinauna ang six-gill shark sa malalim at may extra gill slit—parang shark na may bonus breathing pocket.
basic.es: Los tiburones de seis branquias son nadadores antiguos con una hendidura extra, como un bolsillo de respiración.

edu.en: They rise from deep canyons at night to hunt near the surface.
edu.tl: Umaakyat mula sa malalim na canyon gabi para manghuli sa ibabaw.
edu.es: Suben de cañones profundos de noche a cazar cerca de la superficie.

habitat.tl: Malalim na karagatan at continental slope

region.tl: Temperate na karagatan sa buong mundo

basic.en: Grenadier fish cruise the abyss with giant heads and rat-long tails, looking like a wiggly ribbon that forgot to stop growing.
basic.tl: Lumalangoy sa kailaliman ang grenadier fish na may malaking ulo at buntot na parang daga, parang wiggly ribbon na hindi tumigil sa paglaki.
basic.es: El pez granadero recorre el abismo con cabeza enorme y cola larguísima, como una cinta que se estiró sin parar.

edu.en: Also called rattails; they swim slowly scavenging detritus in deep cold water.
edu.tl: Rattail din ang tawag; dahan-dahang kumakain ng detritus sa malamig na tubig.
edu.es: También 'colas de rata'; carroñean detritos en agua fría.

habitat.tl: Malalim na ilalim ng dagat sa buong mundo

basic.en: Lizardfish bury in sand with only eyes showing, then SNAP—one fast bite turns a passing fish into lunch.
basic.tl: Nagbabaon sa buhangin ang lizardfish at mata lang ang kita, tapos SNAP—isang mabilis na kagat at lunch na ang dumaan na isda.
basic.es: El pez lagarto se entierra en la arena con solo los ojos afuera y luego ¡SNAP!, un mordisco rápido convierte a un pez que pasa en almuerzo.

edu.en: Camouflaged ambush hunters have mouths full of sharp needle teeth.
edu.tl: Ambush hunter na may bibig puno ng matutulis na ngipin.
edu.es: Cazadores de emboscada con bocas llenas de agujas.

heightLength.en: About 4–5 ft long but thin as pencil
heightLength.tl: Mga 4–5 ft pero manipis parang lapis
heightLength.es: Alrededor de 4–5 pies pero delgado como lápiz

basic.en: Snipe eels have beak-like jaws on tiny heads, curving out like deep-sea needles sewing through the dark.
basic.tl: May beak-like jaws ang snipe eel sa maliit na ulo—parang karayom na naghahabi sa dilim ng dagat.
basic.es: Las anguilas picuda tienen mandíbulas en cabezas diminutas, como agujas que cosen la oscuridad del mar profundo.

edu.en: They sweep curved jaws through swarms of shrimp in midwater.
edu.tl: Iniiikot ang baluktot na panga sa kawan ng hipon.
edu.es: Barren mandíbulas curvas en cardúmenes de camarones.

basic.en: Cusk eels hide in muddy burrows like ribbon ninjas, peeking out with wiggly heads to ambush passing snacks.
basic.tl: Nagtatago ang cusk eel sa butas ng putik na parang ribbon ninja, sumisilip lang ang umuugoy na ulo para biglang sunggaban ang meryenda.
basic.es: Las anguilas cusk se esconden en madrigueras de lodo como ninjas de cinta y asoman la cabeza para sorprender a su comida.

edu.en: Some species brood eggs in their mouths or guard them in rocky nests.
edu.tl: May species na nag-iingat ng itlog sa bibig o sa bato.
edu.es: Algunas guardan huevos en la boca o en nidos rocosos.

habitat.tl: Putik sa malalim na ilalim ng dagat

region.tl: Malalim na tubig ng Atlantic at Pacific

basic.en: Chimaeras are “ghost sharks” from deep water, with smooth bodies and strange snouts that make them look like ancient ocean secrets.
basic.tl: Tinatawag na “ghost shark” ang chimaera na nakatira sa malalim na tubig, may makinis na katawan at kakaibang nguso na parang sinaunang sikreto ng dagat.
basic.es: Las quimeras son “tiburones fantasma” de aguas profundas, con cuerpo liso y hocico raro, como un secreto antiguo del océano.

edu.en: Males have retractable forehead claspers used to hold females during mating.
edu.tl: May retractable na clasper sa noo ang lalaki kapag nag-aasawa.
edu.es: Machos tienen pinzas frontales retráctiles al aparearse.

habitat.tl: Malalim na batong ilalim ng dagat

basic.en: Ghost sharks glide through deep reefs with spooky eyes and plate teeth, looking like ancient fish from a midnight story.
basic.tl: Lumalangoy ang ghost shark sa malalim na reef na may nakakakilabot na mata—parang sinaunang isda sa kuwentong gabi.
basic.es: Los tiburones fantasma nadan en arrecifes profundos con ojos misteriosos, como peces antiguos de un cuento nocturno.

edu.en: They are ancient cartilaginous fish related to sharks, older than dinosaurs.
edu.tl: Sinaunang cartilaginous fish na mas matanda pa sa dinosaur.
edu.es: Peces cartilaginosos antiguos, anteriores a dinosaurios.

habitat.tl: Malalim na slope sa ilalim ng dagat

region.tl: Malalim na karagatan sa buong mundo

basic.en: Spookfish have mirror eyes that reflect light upward—built-in periscopes!
basic.tl: May mirror eye ang spookfish na sumasalamin ng liwanag pataas—periscope!
basic.es: ¡Ojos espejo reflejan luz hacia arriba como periscopios!

edu.en: They look both up and down at once to spot prey silhouettes and predators.
edu.tl: Tumingin pataas at pababa sabay para makita ang biktima at predator.
edu.es: Miran arriba y abajo a la vez para presas y depredadores.

region.tl: Tropikal at temperate na malalim na dagat

basic.en: Wolffish look fierce with chunky teeth, but they gently crunch sea urchins like crackers to help the reef.
basic.tl: Mukhang fierce ang wolffish pero dahan-dahang ngangata ng sea urchin na parang cracker para tulungan ang reef.
basic.es: El pez lobo parece feroz, pero mastica erizos de mar como galletas para ayudar al arrecife.

edu.en: They produce antifreeze proteins to survive icy North Atlantic waters.
edu.tl: May antifreeze protein para mabuhay sa malamig na Atlantic.
edu.es: Proteínas anticongelantes en aguas atlánticas frías.

habitat.tl: Malamig na batong ilalim ng dagat

basic.en: Mariana snailfish live deeper than any other fish—almost 27,000 feet down, where the pressure could squish a truck.
basic.tl: Nakatira ang Mariana snailfish nang mas malalim pa sa ibang isda—halos 27,000 feet, kayang pisakin ang truck ng pressure.
basic.es: El pez caracol de las Marianas vive más profundo que cualquier pez, donde la presión aplastaría un camión.

edu.en: Their gelatinous bodies handle crushing pressure of the Challenger Deep.
edu.tl: Kayang tiisin ng gelatinous body ang pressure ng Challenger Deep.
edu.es: Cuerpos gelatinosos soportan presión del Challenger Deep.

habitat.es: Fondo de la Fosa de las Marianas

basic.en: The faceless cusk seems to have no face because its mouth hides underneath, like a deep-sea trapdoor full of surprises.
basic.tl: Parang walang mukha ang faceless cusk dahil nakatago sa ilalim ang bibig, parang deep-sea trapdoor na puno ng sorpresa.
basic.es: El cusk sin cara parece no tener rostro porque su boca está debajo, como una trampilla del mar profundo llena de sorpresas.

edu.en: Found nearly 2.5 miles down, they feed on crustaceans in perpetual blackness.
edu.tl: Natagpuan halos 2.5 milya sa ilalim; kumakain ng crustacean sa dilim.
edu.es: Viven a 2.5 millas de profundidad comiendo crustáceos.

habitat.tl: Malalim na trench sa ilalim ng dagat

basic.en: Black swallowers eat fish twice their size—their stomach stretches like a balloon!
basic.tl: Kumakain ang black swallower ng isdang doble ang laki—lumuluwag ang tiyan!
basic.es: ¡Comen peces del doble de su tamaño; estómago como globo!

edu.en: They hunt in darkness with sharp teeth locking prey so it can't escape.
edu.tl: Nanghuhuli sa dilim na may matutulis na ngipin.
edu.es: Cazan en oscuridad con dientes que atrapan presas.

region.tl: Tropikal at subtropikal na malalim na dagat

basic.en: Deep sea jellies drift like glowing ghosts with tentacles longer than buses!
basic.tl: Lumulutang parang glowing ghost na may tentacles na mas mahaba kaysa bus!
basic.es: ¡Flotan como fantasmas luminosos con tentáculos enormes!

edu.en: Some species are bioluminescent lures in the midnight zone food chain.
edu.tl: May bioluminescent lure sa midnight zone food chain.
edu.es: Señuelos bioluminiscentes en la cadena alimentaria abisal.

basic.en: Comb jellies rainbow-pulse rows of cilia like living fiber-optic ribbons!
basic.tl: Kumikislap ang comb jelly parang fiber-optic ribbon!
basic.es: ¡Filas de cilios pulsan arcoíris como cintas de fibra óptica!

edu.en: Not true jellyfish—they use sticky cells called colloblasts to catch prey.
edu.tl: Hindi tunay na jellyfish—gumagamit ng colloblast para huliin ang biktima.
edu.es: No son medusas; células colloblast pegajosas atrapan presas.

habitat.en: Open ocean surface to deep water

basic.en: Abyssal spiderfish sprawl on the seafloor with long spiny fins, looking like spiders that traded webs for deep-sea chill.
basic.tl: Nakahawak sa sahig ang abyssal spiderfish na may mahabang tinik na fin—parang gagamba sa malalim na dagat.
basic.es: El pez araña abisal yace en el fondo con aletas espinosas largas, como arañas del mar profundo.

edu.en: They sit on the bottom sensing vibrations from falling food in perpetual darkness.
edu.tl: Nakaupo sa ilalim at nararamdaman ang vibration mula sa pagkain na bumababa.
edu.es: Sienten vibraciones de comida que cae en oscuridad perpetua.

basic.en: Beaked whales can dive deeper than almost any mammal on one breath—like holding your nose for a roller-coaster ride to the abyss.
basic.tl: Kayang sumisid ang beaked whale nang napakalalim sa isang hininga—parang roller-coaster pababa sa kailaliman.
basic.es: Las ballenas picudas bucean más profundo que casi cualquier mamífero en una respiración, como una montaña rusa al abismo.

edu.en: They use echolocation clicks to hunt squid in pitch-black trenches.
edu.tl: Echolocation click para manghuli ng pusit sa madilim na trench.
edu.es: Clics de ecolocalización cazan calamares en fosas oscuras.

region.tl: Malalim na karagatan sa buong mundo

basic.en: Big red jellies drift like giant crimson umbrellas in the deep, pulsing slow and spooky through the dark.
basic.tl: Lumulutang ang big red jelly na parang malaking crimson umbrella sa dilim, mabagal at nakakakilabot.
basic.es: Las medusas rojas grandes flotan como paraguas carmesí en la profundidad, pulsando lento y misterioso.

edu.en: They use tentacles to capture bioluminescent prey in midnight zone waters.
edu.tl: Hinuhuli ang bioluminescent prey gamit ang tentacles sa midnight zone.
edu.es: Tentáculos capturan presas bioluminiscentes en la zona de medianoche.

basic.en: Brisingid starfish lift many arms into the current like a feather duster, catching drifting snacks in the deep dark.
basic.tl: Itinataas ng brisingid starfish ang maraming braso sa agos na parang feather duster, sumasalo ng lumulutang na meryenda sa malalim na dilim.
basic.es: Las estrellas brisingidas levantan muchos brazos en la corriente como un plumero, atrapando bocaditos que flotan en la oscuridad profunda.

edu.en: Each arm tip has tiny claws that grab passing plankton in deep cold water.
edu.tl: May maliit na claw sa dulo ng braso para huliin ang plankton.
edu.es: Garras en puntas de brazos atrapan plancton.

basic.en: Flashlight fish have glowing spots under their eyes—they blink lights to talk!
basic.tl: May kumikinang spot sa ilalim ng mata—kumikislap ang ilaw para mag-usap!
basic.es: ¡Puntos brillantes bajo los ojos parpadean para comunicarse!

edu.en: Bioluminescent bacteria live in pouches; fish cover them to hide from predators.
edu.tl: May bacteria sa pouch; tinatakpan para magtago mula sa predator.
edu.es: Bacterias bioluminiscentes en bolsas que ocultan al depredador.

basic.en: Salamanders can regrow legs and tails—superhero amphibians!
basic.tl: Kayang ipagdugtong ng salamander ang binti at buntot—superhero amphibian!
basic.es: ¡Regeneran patas y colas, anfibios superhéroe!

edu.en: Moist skin absorbs oxygen; many live entirely in water as larvae.
edu.tl: Humihinga sa basang balat; marami sa tubig bilang larvae.
edu.es: Piel húmeda respira; muchos acuáticos de jóvenes.

diet.tl: Insekto, uod, at maliliit na isda (karnivoro)

basic.en: Eagles spot tiny prey from skyscraper heights, then dive with talons that grip harder than your strongest handshake.
basic.tl: Nakikita ng agila ang maliit na biktima mula sa napakataas, tapos sumisibol gamit ang talon na mas mahigpit kaysa handshake.
basic.es: Las águilas ven presas diminutas desde alturas enormes y se lanzan con garras más fuertes que un apretón de manos.

edu.en: Nest eyries can weigh a ton; pairs often mate for life.
edu.tl: Maaaring isang tonelada ang nest; mag-asawa habangbuhay.
edu.es: Nidos enormes; a menudo monógamos de por vida.

basic.en: Hummingbirds hover like helicopters and their wings buzz at 80 beats per second!
basic.tl: Lumulutang ang hummingbird at 80 wing beat bawat segundo!
basic.es: ¡Planean a 80 aleteos por segundo!

edu.en: They must eat every few minutes; long tongues lick nectar deep in flowers.
edu.tl: Kailangan kumain bawat ilang minuto; mahaba ang dila sa nectar.
edu.es: Comen cada minutos; lengua larga liba néctar.

basic.en: Cockatiels sport crest mohawks that rise when surprised or happy!
basic.tl: May crest mohawk ang cockatiel kapag gulat o masaya!
basic.es: ¡Moños cresta que se levantan al sorprenderse!

edu.en: Whistling mimics are popular pets from Australian grasslands.
edu.tl: Whistling mimic mula sa parang ng Australia.
edu.es: Silbadores imitadores de pastizales australianos.

basic.en: Parakeets chatter in whistles and beeps, copying sounds like tiny feathered DJs remixing the room all day.
basic.tl: Madaldal ang parakeet sa sipol at beep, kinokopya ang tunog na parang munting feathered DJ na nagre-remix ng buong kwarto.
basic.es: Los periquitos charlan con silbidos y pitidos, copiando sonidos como DJs diminutos con plumas que hacen remix todo el día.

edu.en: Flocks flash green in wild Australia flying fast between trees.
edu.tl: Berde ang kawan sa ligaw na Australia.
edu.es: Bandadas verdes vuelan rápido en Australia.

region.tl: Australia; sikat na alaga sa buong mundo

basic.en: Male cardinals wear bright red feathers like feathered superheroes at feeders!
basic.tl: Matingkad na pula ang lalaking cardinal parang feathered superhero!
basic.es: ¡Machos rojo brillante como superhéroes emplumados!

edu.en: Both parents feed chicks; crest rises when birds alert.
edu.tl: Parehong magulang ang nagpapakain; tumataas ang crest kapag alerto.
edu.es: Ambos padres alimentan crías; cresta se alza al alertar.

basic.en: Blue jays mimic hawk calls to scare other birds away from food!
basic.tl: Gumagaya ang blue jay ng tunog ng lawin para takutin ang ibon!
basic.es: ¡Imitan halcones para espantar aves de comida!

edu.en: Bright blue isn't pigment—barb structure scatters light like magic.
edu.tl: Hindi pigment ang asul—barb structure ang nag-scatter ng liwanag.
edu.es: El azul no es pigmento; estructura de barbas dispersa luz.

basic.en: Pelicans scoop fish in huge bill pouches that hold 3 gallons of water!
basic.tl: Sinisisid ng pelican ang isda sa bill pouch na kasing laki ng 3 galon!
basic.es: ¡Sacosen peces en bolsas de 3 galones!

edu.en: They plunge-dive from heights or swim cooperatively herding fish.
edu.tl: Tumatalon mula sa taas o sabay-sabay humaharang ng isda.
edu.es: Se lanzan desde altura o arrea peces en grupo.

basic.en: Seagulls drop clams on rocks to crack them—smart beach chefs!
basic.tl: Ibinabagsak ng seagull ang clam sa bato para basagin—smart beach chef!
basic.es: ¡Suelen almejas en rocas para abrirlas!

edu.en: They drink salt water using special glands that flush out salt.
edu.tl: Umiinom ng maalat na tubig gamit ang special gland.
edu.es: Beben agua salada con glándulas que eliminan sal.

basic.en: Albatrosses glide for hours without flapping—ocean wanderers with airplane wings!
basic.tl: Lumilipad ng oras nang walang flap—airplane wings ng karagatan!
basic.es: ¡Planean horas sin aletear, alas de avión!

edu.en: They sleep while flying and may not touch land for years.
edu.tl: Natutulog habang lumilipad at taon-taon na hindi bumababa sa lupa.
edu.es: Duermen volando; años sin tocar tierra.

basic.en: Turkey vultures sniff out meals from miles away, doing nature’s cleanup job with wings built for soaring all day.
basic.tl: Naaamoy ng turkey vulture ang pagkain mula sa malayo—nature cleanup crew na lumilipad buong araw.
basic.es: Los zopilotes huelen comida desde lejos y limpian la naturaleza con alas hechas para planear todo el día.

edu.en: Bald red heads stay clean while feeding; they pee on legs to cool off.
edu.tl: Kalbo ang pulang ulo; umiihi sa binti para magpalamig.
edu.es: Cabezas rojas calvas; orinan patas para refrescarse.

basic.en: Penguins can’t fly in air, but they rocket through water like torpedoes, steering with stiff wings and happy feet.
basic.tl: Hindi lumilipad ang penguin sa hangin, pero parang torpedo sa tubig—gumagamit ng matigas na pakpak at masayang paa.
basic.es: Los pingüinos no vuelan en el aire, pero disparan en el agua como torpedos con alas rígidas y patas felices.

edu.en: Dense feathers and blubber keep them warm in icy seas.
edu.tl: Makapal na balahibo at blubber sa malamig na dagat.
edu.es: Plumas densas y grasa en mares helados.

basic.en: Snowy owls hunt in daylight on white wings—Arctic ghosts with yellow eyes!
basic.tl: Magnanakaw sa araw ang snowy owl sa puting pakpak—Arctic ghost!
basic.es: ¡Cazan de día con alas blancas y ojos amarillos!

edu.en: Females have more dark spots; males whiten with age.
edu.tl: Mas maraming batik ang babae; pumuputi ang lalaki habang tumatanda.
edu.es: Hembras más manchadas; machos blanquean con edad.

region.en: Arctic regions; sometimes south in winter
region.tl: Arctic; minsan sa timog sa taglamig
region.es: Ártico; a veces más al sur en invierno

basic.en: Parrots copy sounds, crack puzzles, and laugh like feathered comedians who never forget a favorite word.
basic.tl: Kinokopya ng parrot ang tunog, nalulusutan ang puzzle, at tumatawa na parang feathered comedian na hindi nakakalimot ng salita.
basic.es: Los loros imitan sonidos, resuelven acertijos y ríen como comediantes con plumas que recuerdan sus palabras favoritas.

edu.en: Curved beaks crack nuts; zygodactyl feet grip branches like hands.
edu.tl: Baluktot na tuka at paa parang kamay sa sanga.
edu.es: Picos curvos y patas tipo mano agarran ramas.

region.tl: Tropikal na rehiyon sa buong mundo

basic.en: Macaws flash rainbow feathers and mate for life—flying jewels!
basic.tl: Rainbow feather ang macaw na mag-asawa habangbuhay—flying jewel!
basic.es: ¡Plumas arcoíris y parejas de por vida, joyas voladoras!

edu.en: Powerful beaks open hard palm nuts; they fly miles daily for food.
edu.tl: Matibay ang tuka sa palm nut; milya ang lipad araw-araw.
edu.es: Picos abren nueces de palma; vuelan millas diarias.

heightLength.en: About 2 ft long; bill up to 8 in
heightLength.es: Alrededor de 2 pies; pico hasta 8 pulgadas

basic.en: Toucans have huge colorful bills that look heavy but are light as foam!
basic.tl: Malaki ang makulay na bill ng toucan pero magaan parang foam!
basic.es: ¡Picos enormes coloridos ligeros como espuma!

edu.en: Bills help reach fruit and regulate body heat in tropics.
edu.tl: Tumutulong ang bill abutin ang prutas at magpalamig.
edu.es: Picos alcanzan fruta y disipan calor.

basic.en: Barn owls screech instead of hoot and hear mice under snow!
basic.tl: Sumisigaw ang barn owl imbes na hoot—maririnig ang daga sa ilalim ng niyebe!
basic.es: ¡Chillan en vez de ulular y oyen ratones bajo nieve!

edu.en: Heart-shaped face funnels sound to ears for pinpoint hunting.
edu.tl: Heart-shaped na mukha para i-funnel ang tunog sa tainga.
edu.es: Rostro en corazón concentra sonido para cazar.

basic.en: Great horned owls have ear tufts like horns and can carry prey heavier than themselves!
basic.tl: May ear tuft parang sungay at kaya magdala ng mas mabigat kaysa sarili!
basic.es: ¡Penachos como cuernos y cargan presas más pesadas!

edu.en: Silent flight feathers let them sneak up on rabbits at night.
edu.tl: Tahimik ang pakpak para makalapit sa kuneho gabi.
edu.es: Vuelo silencioso acecha conejos de noche.

basic.en: Border collies are brainy herders that use a laser-stare to guide sheep—like furry air-traffic controllers on the farm.
basic.tl: Matalino ang border collie na parang air-traffic controller sa bukid—titig lang, alam na ng tupa kung saan pupunta.
basic.es: Los border collie guían ovejas con una mirada súper enfocada, como controladores aéreos peludos en la granja.

edu.en: Bred for stamina and focus; they excel at agility and learning tricks.
edu.tl: Para sa tibay at focus; magaling sa agility at tricks.
edu.es: Resistencia y foco; campeones de agilidad.

basic.en: Golden retrievers fetch like champions and greet people with whole-body wags, as if happiness has a tail and fluffy ears.
basic.tl: Mahilig mag-fetch ang golden retriever at bumabati sa pagwag ng buong katawan, parang ang saya mismo ay may buntot at malalambot na tenga.
basic.es: Los golden retrievers traen cosas como campeones y saludan moviendo todo el cuerpo, como si la alegría tuviera cola y orejas peludas.

edu.en: Soft mouths carry eggs without breaking; bred to retrieve waterfowl.
edu.tl: Malambot ang ngatngat para sa itlog at waterfowl.
edu.es: Bocas suaves traen presas sin dañar.

basic.en: Coyotes yip and howl from forests to city parks, proving you can be wild, clever, and still find dinner anywhere.
basic.tl: Umuungal ang coyote mula gubat hanggang park—wild, talino, at laging may mahanap na hapunan.
basic.es: Los coyotes aúllan del bosque al parque, salvajes y listos, encontrando cena en cualquier lugar.

edu.en: They adapt from deserts to downtown eating fruit, pets, and rodents.
edu.tl: Mula disyerto hanggang lungsod—prutas, rodent, at iba pa.
edu.es: Desde desiertos hasta ciudades comen de todo.

basic.en: Wolf pups play tag to learn hunting skills—wild puppies at school!
basic.tl: Naglalaro ang batang wolf ng tag para matutong manghuli—wild puppies sa school!
basic.es: ¡Los cachorros de lobo juegan para aprender a cazar!

edu.en: Pack howls travel miles and help wolves coordinate hunts and reunite families.
edu.tl: Maririnig ang ungol ng pack sa milya-milya at tumutulong mag-coordinate sa pangangaso.
edu.es: Los aullidos del grupo coordinan cacerías a kilómetros.

diet.tl: Usa, elk, at maliliit na mammal (karnivoro)

heightLength.tl: Mga 1 ft ang taas; 2 ft haba
heightLength.es: Alrededor de 1 pie de alto; 2 pies de largo

basic.en: Siamese cats are famous talkers, meowing like they have stories to tell, and their bright eyes make every stare feel dramatic.
basic.tl: Kilala ang Siamese cat sa pagiging madaldal—meow nang meow na parang may kuwento—at ang maliwanag na mata ay parang laging drama ang tingin.
basic.es: Los gatos siameses son muy parlanchines, maúllan como si contaran historias, y sus ojos brillantes vuelven dramática cada mirada.

edu.en: Color points on ears and paws come from temperature-sensitive fur pigment.
edu.tl: Color point sa tainga at paa mula sa temperatura ng balahibo.
edu.es: Puntos de color en orejas y patas por pigmento térmico.

region.tl: Thailand origin; alaga sa buong mundo

heightLength.tl: Mga 1 ft ang taas; 1.5–2 ft haba
heightLength.es: Alrededor de 1 pie de alto; 1.5–2 pies de largo

basic.en: Tabby cats often sport an M-shaped mark on their forehead, like a tiny tiger crown saying ‘king of the couch.’
basic.tl: Kadalasang may M-shaped mark sa noo ang tabby cat—parang tiny tiger crown na hari ng couch.
basic.es: Los gatos atigrados suelen tener una M en la frente, como corona de tigre mini en el sofá.

edu.en: Stripes and swirls are common wildcat patterns found in most house cats.
edu.tl: Guhit at swirl mula sa wildcat pattern ng karamihan sa pusa.
edu.es: Rayas y remolinos de patrones de gatos salvajes.

heightLength.en: About 3 ft at shoulder; 9–10 ft long
heightLength.tl: Mga 3 ft sa balikat; 9–10 ft haba
heightLength.es: Alrededor de 3 pies al hombro; 9–10 pies de largo

basic.en: Bengal tiger stripes are as unique as fingerprints, and a roar can rumble through the air like a giant drumbeat.
basic.tl: Kasing-unique ng fingerprint ang guhit ng Bengal tiger, at ang ungal ay parang higanteng drum na yumayanig sa hangin.
basic.es: Las rayas del tigre de Bengala son tan únicas como huellas, y su rugido retumba en el aire como un tambor gigante.

edu.en: They swim well and patrol huge territories marking trees with scent.
edu.tl: Mahusay lumangoy at minamarkahan ang teritoryo sa puno.
edu.es: Nadan bien y marcan árboles en vastos territorios.

basic.en: Bobcats have short bobbed tails and tufted ears, sneaking through forests like mini lynxes on tiptoe.
basic.tl: Maikli ang buntot ng bobcat at may tufted ears—parang mini lynx na tiptoe sa gubat.
basic.es: Los gatos monteses tienen cola corta y orejas con mechones, deslizándose como linces pequeños de puntillas.

edu.en: Stealthy hunters that pounce up to 10 ft to catch rabbits and birds.
edu.tl: Tahimik na mangangaso na tumatalon ng 10 ft para habulin ang kuneho at ibon.
edu.es: Cazan sigilosos con saltos de 10 pies tras conejos y aves.

basic.en: Lynx have snowshoe paws and black ear tufts like built-in antennae!
basic.tl: Parang snowshoe ang paa ng lynx at may itim na tuft sa tainga parang antenna!
basic.es: ¡Patas de raqueta y mechones negros en orejas!

edu.en: Huge paws spread weight on snow while hunting snowshoe hares.
edu.tl: Kumakalat ang malaking paa sa niyebe habang hinahabol ang snowshoe hare.
edu.es: Patrullan nieve persiguiendo liebres de raqueta.

basic.en: Leopards drag prey up trees—dinner with a view away from lions!
basic.tl: Hinihila ng leopard ang biktima sa puno—hapunan na may view malayo sa leon!
basic.es: ¡Suben presas a árboles lejos de leones!

edu.en: Spotted coats break up their outline, hiding them in dappled forest light.
edu.tl: Nagkakamuflage ang batik sa liwanag ng gubat.
edu.es: Las manchas los camuflan en luz moteada del bosque.

basic.en: Jaguars have rosette spots and bite so strong they crack turtle shells!
basic.tl: May rosette spot ang jaguar at kayang durugin ang shell ng pagong!
basic.es: ¡Manchas en roseta y mordida que rompe caparazones!

edu.en: They are the only big cats that regularly live in Americas rainforests.
edu.tl: Sila lang ang malaking pusa na regular sa rainforest ng Americas.
edu.es: Son los únicos grandes felinos habituales de selvas americanas.

basic.en: Some moths wear disguise wings that copy wasps or owl eyes, throwing a costume party to trick hungry hunters.
basic.tl: May moth na naka-costume na pakpak na parang wasp o mata ng kuwago—parang party ng disguise para lokohin ang hunter.
basic.es: Algunas polillas tienen alas disfrazadas de avispa u ojos de búho, como fiesta de disfraces para engañar depredadores.

edu.en: Silk moths spin cocoons; adults often don't eat, living only to mate.
edu.tl: Gumagawa ng cocoon ang silk moth; hindi kumakain ang adulto.
edu.es: Polillas de seda en capullos; adultos a veces no comen.

diet.en: Nectar or none as adults; larvae eat leaves (varies)
diet.tl: Nectar o wala bilang adulto; dahon ang larvae (nag-iiba)
diet.es: Néctar o nada de adultos; larvas comen hojas (varía)

basic.en: Tarantulas are gentle giants that flick itchy hairs when annoyed!
basic.tl: Maamo ang tarantula pero nagfi-flick ng makati na balahibo kapag naiinis!
basic.es: ¡Gigantes gentiles lanzan pelos urticantes!

edu.en: They molt exoskeletons to grow and can live decades.
edu.tl: Nagbabago ng exoskeleton para lumaki; maaaring dekada ang buhay.
edu.es: Mudan exoesqueletos; viven décadas.

basic.en: Fireflies blink bioluminescent codes to find friends on summer nights!
basic.tl: Kumikislap ang firefly para hanapin ang kaibigan sa gabing tag-init!
basic.es: ¡Destellos bioluminiscentes buscan amigos en verano!

edu.en: Light comes from a chemical reaction in the abdomen, mostly in males signaling.
edu.tl: Chemical reaction sa tiyan ang ilaw, lalo na sa lalaki.
edu.es: Luz por reacción química en abdomen, sobre todo machos.

diet.en: Nectar and pollen (omnivore); larvae eat snails
diet.tl: Nectar at pollen (omniboro); snail ang larvae
diet.es: Néctar y polen (omnívoro); larvas comen caracoles

basic.en: Leafcutter ants march carrying leaf pieces like tiny green sails!
basic.tl: Naglalakad ang leafcutter ant na may dahon parang green sail!
basic.es: ¡Marchan con trozos de hoja como velas verdes!

edu.en: They don't eat leaves—they farm fungus on them underground.
edu.tl: Hindi kumakain ng dahon—nagfa-farm ng fungus sa ilalim.
edu.es: No comen hojas; cultivan hongos subterráneos.

basic.en: A turtle carries its “house” on its back and can live for decades, making slow adventures that outlast many people.
basic.tl: Bitbit ng pagong ang “bahay” sa likod at nabubuhay nang maraming dekada, kaya ang mabagal na adventure ay tumatagal nang sobra.
basic.es: La tortuga carga su “casa” en la espalda y puede vivir por décadas, haciendo aventuras lentas que duran más que muchas personas.

edu.en: Breathe air with lungs; some hibernate underwater months in mud.
edu.tl: Humihinga sa hangin; nag-hibernate sa ilalim ng tubig sa putik.
edu.es: Respiran aire; algunas hibernan bajo agua en lodo.

region.tl: Buong mundo maliban sa pinaka-malamig na polo
